Description: Biere Club is a fun place to hang out on a weekend or after a long day at work!  if you're the kinda person who doesn't like stuffy places, get a seat by the balcony and order that Malibu - it tastes great!!

Author: Atash Patel
Description:
I liked this pub....good old world vibe, with classic rock playing in the background, friendly banter and chatter all over, giving it a "mondegarish" kind of feel...(mumbaikars would know what I am talking about....)

Though a bit congested, its a haven of a place on a -so-expensive location like lavelle road. The drinks are surprisingly quite reasonable, almost unexpected of in Lavelle road. Though the beer is somewhat watery.
Loved the potato wedges served, ..nice crisp and hot. Could not say the same about the chicken wings though, they were too sticky to my taste.

This is a popular joint, and looks like its popularity is gonna stay!!!

Author: Ranjit Paul
Description: Visited the place with a friend of mine. The place was full even on a weekday. The music was nice, although I found it to be a tag loud. My friend ordered the shandy lager lemonade. It was a beer based cocktail which he really liked even though he is not a beer person. I tried the stout which I really enjoyed, good dark beer. For food we had nachos and potato wedges, good but nothing extraordinary to talk about.

Author: Neelav Samrat De
Description:
Visited this other microbrewery near UB City today with a friend. Must say, Bangalore has way better places than Delhi NCR!
This is an awesome pub which has 3 floors and an excellent ambience. The second floor is open on 2 sides and the bar is located here and the ground floor floor as well.
Love the Bangalore pub scene. Very modern, hip, chic and great crowd. The Biere Club is one such place.
The service is a tad slow and you need to wave your hands frantically to grab the attention of an attendant to reorder your beer or ask for the next dish.
Whatever we ordered was excellent in presentation and taste. The wheat beer was great!
The golden mushroom fry came in a dish with red chili sauce on the base of the plate. We ordered baked potatoes with pepper and garnish. This was good too.
I loved this place. It was good and not very expensive too.
Overall rating would be an easy 4 on 5. Would recommend anyone to visit it for sure.
Excellento!

Author: Prakhar Jain
Description: Much hyped a place..had heard a lot from friends so definitely wanted to visit. However, didn't find it that exciting. The beer was good, found the food ordinary though. The service was good, the staff is courteous and prompt. If you're fond of microbreweries, you definitely want to be here once, however I'd rate my experience at TOIT better.

Author: Kalyani Kulkarni
Description:
The food ALSO is awesome!
Must Try - Pigs In The Blanket!!!! Lip-smacking!

Author: Thotathil Fredy Abraham Sabu
Description: Searching for the best ale beer in bangalore ?.... This is where u should be !!!

Author: Ajay Kumar
Description: Excellent Beer thought didn't like the food much...

Author: Shivram Subramanian
Description:
Frankly this was my first visit to this Brewery in Bangalore (had been to one in Pune).

Its a great place for beers. You have to try one called (not sure if I remember the name correctly) Whisky Beer. It is a superb one. I am not going to reveal how it is made or any of the amazing details. Just go and try it out! And also make sure you try out their beer of the day.

However, a small note for the veggies, you may not get many variety. For the non-veg lovers, it is filled with some yummy starters that will get you drooling.

The ambience is superb. Great music and good food...All that you would want especially after a long Friday. :D

Cheers!

Author: Purva Joshi
Description:
Okay, so my first visit to B'lore as an Adult and out of the innumerable tempting options of pubs and discs finally this is the place where we all ended up. Having said that, I must say the experience was far from disappointing.
It was a Saturday Night so the crowd was obvious but I was thrilled to see that every floor of this 3-storied building was bustling with cheerful and jolly middle-aged youngsters joking, laughing loudly, eating and drinking which added a whole 'happy mood' to the place. Though I didn't eat much, the black velvet cocktail and risotto fritters with balsamic mayo distinctly stood out. Fresh Beer Pints were nice and overall had a great time with friends :)

Author: Dr Asad Chaturvedi
Description:
One of the micro-breweries that i actually like because of the variety in food and the amazing brews on the beers. They serve a platter which is amazing and has a variety of starters from different parts of the world. Further if you are a Pizza lover then try out their pizza which has chorizo as one of the toppings. It is to die for.

The brews are so good that one of the gang who is a total teetollar took the plunge and downed not one but two. Lovely place to hang out with friends and family alike as they have a family section.

Would not recommend this place if you are thinking on budgetary lines as its quite expensive. So if you do go please indulge and forget worrying about the bill coz either-ways its going to be steep.

Author: Gaurav Vartak
Description: Visited this place during a weekday with some friends. Excellent beer. Loved the Wheat beer. Ale was good too.

Author: Dilin Anand
Description:
I had got a lot of positive reviews about this place, and had come on request of a friend. I however must say that to me, it wasn't all it was hyped up to be. The brew was good, but the food served was .. meh.

The worst part was the flies, there were loads of them! My buddy got two beers replaced cuz of a fly falling in them. The staff was nice enough to refill without charging but I wasn't able to look at my beer the same way again.

Author: Anant Gupta
Description:
This place is simply awesome.
Exotic location: nearby UB City and theres a Harley showroom behind.
The ambience is so perfect!
The food is crazily expensive but does leave you craving for more.
Do have the chicken sausage wrapped in bacon.

Must visit place if you're in Bangalore.

Author: Mayank Mehta
Description:
It is beautifully located in one of the posh localities of Bangalore making it readily accessible. It is pretty difficult to have a perfect blend of indoor and outdoor sitting, this pub does it best. It has plenty of screens so it makes Biere Club one of the happening places during this football season.

The beer collection they serve is pretty good and smooth, I can't say much about their cocktails as I went there only for their beers. What i can say though is, i was not disappointed. The service was pretty slow but the maybe that's because of the huge crowd that walks in during weekends.

The food was a bit disappointing though, the veg and non-veg platter needs re-planning and lot of thought. Their main course was better off though. This place fare's well in the ambiance and beers but is a let down in food and service. Having said that, its definitely worth going to.

Author: Zimit
Description:
Located in the most upmarket address of the city and right opposite to UB City, it attracts great crowd and manages to rake in good moolah too.

The space is huge, and is packed to the brim on weekends. The ambience is nice and is of good quality.

The beer is pretty decent, but it can be better. The options it offers are nice and good enough.

The let down is the food, the portions are less, the pricing is not apt and the quality is not consistent. It definitely needs to improve on that front.

A good place overall, the kind of crowd it attracts and the location is its USP.

Author: Anupam Bose
Description:
Biere Club is one of the first microbreweries in Bangalore. Location is Lavelle Road. Need i say anything more?

Its one of the places that you can visit time and again and you will come back for more. This place is very lively and has a old world charm to it. Music is not very high so ideal place to catch up with friends.I like this place more because you can drop by in the middle of the week and grab a mug of the the biere. The place,energy and the charm is a stress buster and it a feel good factor here always! Food is very good and pairs a lot of english and middle eastern flavors. Love this place for what it is and what it has done for the biere lovers!!

Author: Yash Ladia
Description: I went there just to try a crafted beer.  But I ended up ordering a pitcher if Ale beer which mind you was awesome.  I had chicken drumsticks with it which was good too. Awesome ambience..  I was enjoying the music too.. Coldplay yellow quite matched up with the mood. Nice experience :)

Author: Abraham Chacko
Description: The Biere (Beer) is awesome... The pizza is awesome, the ambience, location and everything are awesome.

Author: Sachin Upot
Description: A great place for Beer enthusiasts. Ambiance and service was perfect.

Author: Shaireen Dhingra Jenveja
Description: Food choices are very limted...thought ambience is good.

Author: Unni
Description: Being there almost 4-5 months back. This is one beer pub I would definitely want to visit again. Good menu choice and excellent beer...Good ambience and ya please be on the 2nd floor

Author: Pradyumn Agarwal
Description:
A nice place to sit down and relax. Had to wait for 10 mins to get a table. The staff was very friendly and the food service was good. Try the mushroom pasta it was amazing with the cheesy and creamy mushroom flavor. Also try the white onion, sliced potato thyme pizza as well. These were the two dishes i liked (5/5). Overall, the ambiance was good but the music was lousy. They could have played a better collection of music.

Food 5/5
Ambiance 4/5
Service 4.5/5
Music 1/5

Author: Madikallolli
Description:
The Beer Walk !

I happened to go to this restaurant on a Saturday. At first we were obliged to sit in front of the beer tanks, then moved to the smoking free zone floor.

We ordered a wheat and samson beer.
It was a mixture of bitter and sweet flavour.
Got slightly high and it was the right time to fuel in for more and eat the right savories.
We ordered lamb starters and marinated beef for starters, which was average.

To finish of the meal we ordered grilled chicken pizza with 2 large jugs of beer, which was a good relishing combo.

And finally u mite be wondering whats with the beer walk, thats coz we ended up walking for 6 kms way back to my home with the fuel we sucked upon! Indeed a good fuel for a night walk!!

Author: Surbhi Tiwari
Description:
A nice place to have very smooth and interesting beers :) .. With pathetic food though ...

I had tried stuffed mushroom which came in a very less quantity compared to price we paid .. But the smooth wheat beer made up for it ..

Have heard the breakfast there us worth trying . Have not tried yet.

Author: Princy
Description: What a crowded place this place is. Always full. You are lucky if you get a place during weekends. Good crowd, good music and good beer. Salads are shockingly good too. Food is really good but a bit pricey. The upper floor is pretty subtle with candlelight and stuff. Good place to chill.

Author: Varun Agarwal
Description:
One of the first breweries in Bangalore, this is again an awesome place to visit. Located on Lavelle Road, it has an cool ambiance & decor, with amazing crowd especially over the weekend.
Each floor here has a variety and class of its own.
The ground floor is the brewery and filled with all the beer lovers. The first floor has a different menu and does not serve fish and chips or the regulars of a pub. Enter this floor for a fine dining experience.
But if you want a more happening place then visit the second floor and you will see a different world altogether.

The Club Special & Stout are the recommended in-house beers here. The service is good here.

Ambiance 4/5
Beer 3.5/5
Food 3/5
Service 4/5

Author: Mahima Singh
Description:
Ok, may be i chose a wrong day to make a visit there.... IPL Finals.... it was hell crowded.
For like 40 mins we were struggling to stand at the bar, forget proper seating...
Screen was far away from us and so was the bartender :(
We ordered a bucket of french fries and THE BIERE CLUB POM MARTINI. That helped us to get charged up a lil bit.
Finally, We got a perfect seating; right in front of the screen n then the night begun ;)

U must try Jagermeister shots, they were just out of this world. Enjoyed it even more as it has an offer running BUY 2 GET 1 :D
Overall it is for sure not " the most happening pub" in town but yes, to catch up with frnds over a large spread of beer... this is the place to be.

Author: Amit Singh
Description: The beer is AWESOME. one of the best microbrew in town. If you are a beer person then do visit this place. Its not very expensive either and the ambience is also pretty nice and casual. Loses points on the food. The food is just average but the beer makes up for it. We happened to visit on a Wednesday and it was a quiz night. The host ( name I can't remember) was really good too. Really funny dude, made the evening more enjoyable.

Author: Prashant Toshniwal
Description:
Been to biere club few times.
Decent place and decent beers, with parking being a major discomfort specially if you are on a bike.
Would rate the place better of they have better parking.

Author: Foodie Che
Description:
I have been to the Biere Club a couple of times earlier and had a pretty decent experience everytime.

BUT this last visit of mine was probably the worst experience.

I arrived around noon and ordered for a regular beer and a non-veg platter.

The fish and the Prawans were just soaked in oil and took out. I dont think they used any corn flour but it looked just like it was dipped in Maida and took out like our normal pakori's..

The entire platter was bad except for the chicken.

ANOTHER BIG SHOCKER FOR ME WAS THERE WERE FLIES ALL OVER THE PLACE.

I called the waiter a couple of times but no one did anything about it.

They were flying and crawling all over the food and me.

Attached some of the pictures.

On an average
Service 4/5
Quality of food 2.5/5
Ambience - 4/5
Hygiene - 1/5

Author: Ashish Bansal
Description:
Very bad service. Damn rude waiters.Either that or I have some bad luck with the service. Been there twice, once with family, and the waiters are forgetful. Asked 3 times, 3 times for a stout beer and still didn't get it. I would give a 1 out of 10 for the service.
Had encountered a group of flies throughout the time I spent here during the afternoon with my family. You can't really help that, but considering this was in the ground floor makes it ridiculous.
The food however is really amazing.
- The non veg platter is just packed with an amazing variety of food.
-The Mediterranean pizza was good too
-The chicken and artichoke pasta was delicious
. The only reason I gave it a 2.5 is cause of the food.
The top terrace area is much better than the ground floor. It's a good way to relax with a nice glass of chilled beer.
However it's to hot for the summers.

Author: Satwinder Singh
Description:
A nice place.. Was here this Sunday for a some beers and dinner. Surely a small change from the regular.

We took the first floor and were kinda surprised with music that was barely audible and some fantastic beer.

Some starters were ordered. With quantities that were barely enough for 1.. In the sense that most dishes turned out absolutely fantastic, very rich where you would not want to have more than a piece or two for taste and flavor.
The first floor has a different menu and does not serve fish and chips or the regulars of a pub. Enter this floor for its ambiance and silence if you want a peaceful evening. Almost fine dining I would say.

But if you want a more happening place then hit the second floor and you will see a different world altogether.

All in all, surely a good experience and the next time I'm hitting the top floor.

Attention to the friends serving us.. They are doing a great job. But would say to them that please keep your cell phones at bay.. That's why I had to subtract a point.

Best of Luck and keep up the Service, Food and Ambiance.

Author: Jitendra Kumar
Description: Best place to hang out wid frnds

Author: Amit Karnik
Description: Great place if ua fed up of Lager and Drought beers!

Author: Abhijit Kulkarni
Description:
In my opinion this is an average pub. If this was the first micro brewery in town, I would have rated it higher. But it's not. There are others far better. And unfortunately it doesn't match up to the other popular ones like Toit or Barleyz.

The place is conveniently located. Easy to find and in the middle of the city. The beer making apparatus looks great from outside, but the smell of barley and hops hit you as soon as you step in, and stays with you as long as you are there. I guess you need it more ventilated in the ground level. The ambience is like a proper pub, and the menu has lots of pub food. Unfortunately not as many mains as I would have liked.

Had a wheat beer, which was good. But I didn't understand the part where I was told to wait for 20 mins for any other beer. Could not understand if there was a wait for the whole batch to be brewed or they brew beer only when the order comes in. And the food was really okay, no salt was a common feature. And apparently you need to ask 3 different waiters to get a bowl of sauce. Same with salt, or paper napkins.

So if I have to compare this to the hustle and bustle of Toit, for example, and add to that the ambiance and the food choice, I'd say The Biere Club falls short. Their beer might be good, but they need to step up on food and service.

Author: Dechen Wangdi Lachungpa
Description: Undoubtedly my favourite place in Bangalore. Great beer, great ambience. The Pizza is also extremely tasty. Reccomend sitting in the top floor.

Author: Uday Prakash
Description:
Lovely crowd
Long waits to order
Loud music
Have to make reservations
Have to turn AC on
Have a lot of have nots

Author: Mithun M Jagadeesh
Description: We ordered a stout, a club special and a kebab platter the presentation was very good and food was average. Beer was brewed to perfection. Service was really very bad. Server didn't know how to pour beer he spilt on the floor and then another came and served it. And one more annoying thing was house fly it was wandering around us the whole time. Beer and food was good but service is bad. Try to improve your service.

Author: Sandeep Chinnam
Description: The microbrewery right in front of UB city, Perfect place to unwind after a hectic day of shopping. The many in house brews is worth trying out and the starters are large in proportions!

Author: Amrit Poon
Description: If you are a true beer lover, then this is one of those places which you can't miss going to. They usually have 5-6 different types of brewed beers on tap at one point of time. Noons can sometimes be better time to visit the place for a brunch and have a few gulps of freshly brewed beer.

Author: Yograj Patel
Description:
Its a class above.... A worth visiting place to spend some cool time with a chilled Beer. If you are a beer lover then the best place to hangout.

Try the Beer sample to taste all the different flavours available.
The ambience was excellent with roof top and the service was excellent.

Here comes the fooodddd.... We had ordered jumbo platter which was amazing. Its definitely a come again place.

Author: Shreejata Gupta
Description:
A chilled-out place to hang out with your friends. I love the location of the place, and from the first floor, when you look into the street, you feel as if you are sitting in one of those cozy street-corner restaurants in an unknown little town of Europe.

The beer quality is good, however a couple of them on the menu were unavailable on the particular ay I visited. Food was good, but nothing special. The biggest advantage is that they allow smoking inside on the first floor.

The only thing that I would like to change about the first-floor sitting arrangement of the Bierre Club is that there could be more space in between each table. One feels slightly tangled-in after the crowd starts pouring in.

I shall visit shortly!

Author: Nameesh (Fodyssey)
Description:
This accordingly to me is the best Brew Pub in Bangalore because for the complete packages of drink + food + ambience + good crowd :)

I started going here multiple times to meet my college friend. We used to hang-out at Mocha (which was closed) or Biere Club. The first thing you would notice about the place is that kind of crowd that comes there. It is pretty neat crowd and has a equal mix of Indians & Foreigners. Youngsters and working professionals as well. So, you would find very much comfortable going there and grabbing a drink or two.

About the variety of drinks. I think the beer is the best option, especially the Stout-Beer, Wheat Beer. Both of them are simply awesome to taste. You can enjoy that while you get good breeze if you are sitting in the 2nd floor. But, if you are sitting in the ground floor, still it is pretty nice.

Apart from that you should try this drink called 'Flame'. I tried that when I had gone with couple of my colleagues. This is something of a speciality where in the bartender would have 3-4 wines glasses kept facing up and down and again up and down. They are positioned in such a way that the drink poured from top would reach the bottom glass. And he pours the tequilla form the top glass and light up with fire. There is a straw given to you which you need sip the drink from the bottom glass as it trickles down. And must say it is an awesome drink, quite hot and hits the throat but surely worth it :)

I guess drink is not the only thing for which Im rating above 4. If you are a veggie, you get this veggie-platter which is simply simply awesome where they serve you quite a few items including - Crostinin, Fries, Hummus falafal, Olives, Mozerilla with sun-dried tomatoes served with cream, salsa sauce and some salads. I think this is one of the best platter you can eat. And I just love it out there.

It is also not just a place to hang-out. I have gone there with family probably take a drink and have some of the nice food that is there for the offering. You can see families also coming in there. So, overall it is an awesome place to visit. The only problem is in getting the sitting space there.

My overall rating is 4.5 for this place -
Drinks - 4/5
Food - 4.5/5
Service - 4.5/5
Ambience - 4/5

Author: Ankur Jacob
Description:
A very Crowded place on a Saturday evening. I was lucky enough to find a bar stool at the counter. Many people were just standing inside waiting for a table while having their drinks

Their Hefeweizen brew was very good. Service was slow (due to the crowd) but food was delicious

Author: Sidharth Sharma
Description:
After coming to Bangalore I was all excited to explore the city night life.Bangalore was given the status of "party city" back in British times.The question was is it still the same or "Bengaluru" is changed.Me and my friends were staying near to UB city,we started searching nearby clubs to relax after all the hustle bustle during the day,we landed in The Biere Club.
I always prefer to taste all the beer in any brewery before making the final call.So we ordered The Club Special.I must say the it was really Zippy, had good amount of laces and adequate carbonation.
It really gives good street side view,we were enjoy the chilled beer with the people passing by. We were sitting at the open top area.The staff was really courteous and soft spoken.The ambiance was classy from every corner.
The rates were moderate. The food was good but the portions were less.I would love to go again.

Author: Dr Patil
Description:
I can never forget this place , I throned a party to 25 member group of my friends ..Place was awesome it was made for beer lovers alone , I love the pizza here any day ...

Wheat beer was that everyone had that day , but found all others to be good too. They also have nice beer cocktails, like coffee liqueur or spicy tequila added to it which my friends tired it....

What makes this different from many other places that the food is also good - both starters and main courses. And when I say good, I mean really good. No wonder the first floor has become a fine dine restaurant.

The place is large, but still it becomes difficult to find a table on the weekend. 30-45 min wait is usual on friday and saturday evenings....

Author: Swagatika Priyadarshini
Description: Just around the corner on Vittal Malya road, the Biere Club is a good place to hang out with buddies over awesome freshly brewed beer. The thing that stands out is the Beer. I just love their beer, and if I have to compare, I do find it better than Toit. Food is ok, nothing extraordinary. Platters are decent. The ambiance goes with the name of the pub, its really chilled out, nothing sophisticated, just the place to be for Beer. Their seasonal beers are good trys, apart from their classic ale. They have these hangover breakfasts on weekends: delightful European breakfast definitely lifts up the whole weekend mood :)

Author: Nataraj Srikantaiah
Description:
I have heard complaints from my friends about the service but as per my recent experience, on a busy Saturday night the service was excellent.

And I liked the brain hemorrhage shot which was suggested by the tapster and it was really awesome. I will go back at least to have shots :p

Beers were really good, food was average, typical bar snacks, nothing worth the price also the portion what they serve is too less.

Author: Aaish_foodoholic
Description:
Nice place but very over-crowded on Weekends!
The lighting is a bit depressing, if your company isn't right..
However, the most cool and chilled-out crowd of bangalore can be found here!
The food is Good.
Drinks are great!
The best thing about the place is it's Ambiance! :D

Would love to visit again!

Author: Lekha M
Description:
I met up with some friends at Beirre Club on a Wednesday night and I was surprised to see that the place was packed, with the ground floor full and the second floor (terrace) was 85% full. Surprisingly though, the ground floor was entirely cleared out by 10:15pm. There were mainly office going people, unwinding with some beer and snacks. There were barely any youngsters around.

This is one of the first micro breweries that started in Bangalore and its location on Lavelle Road (centre of the city) attracts people from all over the city. My friends ordered the wheat beer pitcher which they seemed to enjoy because they called for another one as soon as it was over. Their beer mugs are huge and looks like it could hold half a litre of beer.

Their assorted fries are very yummy and it is served in small green buckets, along with a dip. We also ordered the beer battered assorted vegetables (which also comes in a green bucket) which was a bit too oily for my taste. They reminded me of south Indian Bhajjis. I could not taste any beer flavour in the batter either.

The main course is well made. The four cheese pasta was very impressive. Penne and a white cheese sauce with more cheese on top, was well balanced and had a nice simple flavour. We also ordered a paneer dish. The paneer was well liked by all my friends and they say it was very soft.

Service was slightly slow. We had to tell the waiter more than once to get more mugs and and some more chairs to accommodate the friends who joined us a bit later.

Overall, the food was well made with a fine taste and the ambience was exciting too, with lots of chitter chatter along the music they played in the background.

Author: Neil Bose
Description:
Overview - well the name is well done...intriguing and welcoming to all the biere lovers... !A good place to have a laid back evening with your friends...not exactly the posh and bourgeois !

Ambiance - 6/10 - nothing out of the ordinary...a big 3 strory buidling...the breweries look nive although !

Food - 3/10 - the platters were rather miniature...extremely less qty and nothing great ...we tried the non-veg mixed kebab platter... and it was...well...not very tempting to eat !oh and yes...there are other places with better freshly brewed beers available... !

Service - 5/10 - no personalisation,no interaction and they seem rather lethargic to even explain to you what myt be what !

Recommendations - go only if you really enjoy beer a lot...they should do something about the smoking open area...i mean...add in a bit more fans/coolers...a/c s dont work during summers !

Author: Shwetha K
Description:
Good place to hangout keeping the locality in mind
Looks Bit boring on the g floor like a family restaurant however the top floors are better.

I like the Whitefield one better.

Author: Manish Bansal
Description: Good ambiance with good staff. Spend nice time. Lovely food great feeling

Author: Shivam Bharadwaj
Description:
The Biere Club, located in the posh vittal mallaya road and is an excellent example of Bangalore' s pub culture. It is famous for beers and lives up to it's name serving EXCELLENT Home made beers from a brewing machine which will make it in front of you. It has a variety of over 10/12 beers made in house and served chilled directly from tap, old style. Their wheat beer is the best of them all and is a must try having a very distinct taste with a sweetish aftertaste. One unique thing is that they have an option of beer samplers, which is a 60 ml shot glass having a tastikng amount of their beers which will enable you to taste their brews beforehand. Apart from in house beers which are reasonably priced, they also have a good range of whiskies, rum, liquers and wines, served at proper temperature and in style. Apart from plain hard drinks they also have a good range of beer cocktails, which can be followed by a steakhouse situated above. Overall, this place wins hands down as far as beer n drinks are concerned, the only drawback being that it is quiet crowded so you might nor be able to listen to music. They have earned a loyal Customer in me and I will surely go back, if nothing else but for their beers.
Total cost. - Rs. 1150 for one 330 ml beer, 2 pegs of irish n bourbon whisky n malibu liqueur n tasting amount f american pale ale.

Author: Ankur Aneja
Description: I went to this place after checking out the review on zomato. The food was good, the drinks were no less but the ambience was not as i expected. The air conditioning had some problem and to top the hot conditions inside the service was poor. The staff was unresponsive to our requests.

Author: Rahul
Description: One of the best or probably the Best place in Bangalore to sit down and have a brilliant gulp of Beer! Love the Ale and the wheat Beer! Must admit they have the best beer in Town, Only Toit comes close to this! The place is good! Gives you the old British feel with a touch of contemporary feel attached to it. Service is very SO SO though! You get a feel the Bartenders are the owners of the place and they are serving Alcohol free of cost!! Yikes! The place gets mental on Fridays and Saturdays. The sooner you go the better it is for you to find a spot to stand. Not a place to go if you like watching live matches. The food is good, the Pizza's in particular. Although the fact remains you gotta eat what they give you as all the items on the menu aren't really available. The cocktails are pretty normal and coming to pricing, its pretty much on the same scale to that of Toit and Arbor Brewing Company. Just go there for the Beers and you wouldn't regret it! The location adds to a better touch. Love the place, if only the service gets better. Will i visit again? Hell yes!! I might even forget the service is ridiculous for the Beers they give you! They probably know the fact too which may be why the service is bad!! ha ha! Go ahead and try the place out!

Author: F1dotcom
Description:
My first visit to this place was almost a year ago when I was visiting Bangalore and met a childhood friend for dinner. He suggested this place as it was supposedly the first microbrewery in India and since he knew that I love Beer, there was no other choice.

He called ahead and asked for a reservation. When we arrived, we realized how packed the place was. Thanks to the reservations already made, we made our way upstairs and into the Air Conditioned area where we got some respite from the summer heat (even though it was night).

Tried and tasted their inhouse brews (Pilsner, Wheat, Ale and Stout - dont remember the exact names though). I am a fan of Pilsner and Lager so I opted for the Pilsner. Service is quick and efficient. The beers tasted much much better than the bottled versions that are available throughout.

For snacks, we tried their Moroccan Spiced Chicken Kebabs and their Chicken Shawarma. Both of them were very authentic and they tastes just like the real authentic thing.

This is one place that I visit each and every time I visit Bangalore. Wish more and more microbreweries open up in India.

Author: Anand
Description:
Go for the ambience and crowd...too good
Beer is real nice and food is awesome

Author: Anusha
Description: Beer Lovers ! This is the place to be !

Author: Food Explorer
Description:
I went to The Biere Club with expectations after reading the positive reviews in Zomato. Unfortunately, I had a very average experience. The ambience was not as good as I thought it would be and that was a little disappointing. We sat at a table on the ground floor. There was a group of people behind us who were pretty noisy and who brought their small kids to the place! The kids were running around and making sounds. I found it really weird that of all the places in Bangalore, these people had to bring their kids to Biere Club. Well I guess stranger things do happen in life!

Coming to the food, I ordered a virgin sangria which was quite good, while my friend ordered beer tasting sampler which consisted of samples of 6 different types of beer. Now from the looks of it, he did not enjoy it much. For starters, we ordered the cheese garlic mushroom and roasted potato. The cheese garlic mushrooms were good while the roasted potato was above average.

We were really hungry and were craving to have a pizza. As bad luck would have it, the waiter told us that their oven was not working and they could not serve pizzas. Well that irritated us and since we did not see any interesting desserts we decided that it was better to ask for the bill and leave!

The beer sampler, mocktail, and two starters added up to 1,300k which was not that reasonable either! So the overall experience was not that great and I would definitely not be going back there again.

Author: Ashish Gupta
Description: Love this place.. One of the best beers in Bangalore.. Getting place is difficult on weekends.. Need to plan in advance even for smaller groups

Author: Utkarsh Mankad
Description:
Me and group of my friends have this penchant to try all the home brewery places in Bangalore. We went to few brewery's here, the Prost, the Arbour brewing company and lately The Toit. The next on the list was this one.

Just like other places, we went here and ordered a set of all the home brew beers. Dark and bitter beers are my favorites, i was looking for mind blowing beer here. And trust me it actually was. My friends tried the lager, wheat and light beers, they also loved it a lot. :)

The food was good, and it went good with the dinner. We ordered veg and non-veg platter, the paneer and chicken both were good and not chewy. For main course we had ordered pizza which was also very good, along with Pesto pasta. The pasta was rich in cheese and very delicious.

The music they play is very popular English numbers which add on to the fun. The service is also prompt.

The place is happening 7 days a week, so mind you do your booking before. The place is pretty huge, so it can fit lot of people.The ambience is also like that of rusty old drink joint as in Hollywood movies. Which i loved.

Author: Chaitanya Shroff
Description:
Huge place, big mugs and so they always talk about pitchers, whether its beer, cocktail or mocktail! Though the starters' portions are less compared to other brewskies, they are full on loaded. Like the beer battered fries, which tastes so awesome, must try. They have tried to fry mushroom and stuff with cheese, but it tasted weird! Off the brewed ones, I have had Ale and Lager, both of them good. Tried the mezze platter, doesn't matter you're a vegetarian or non-vegetarian, you'll have to love it. Did not like the shawarma rolls so much though.

Service is great and the staff were helpful in getting us the table we needed. If you party hard on Friday or Saturday, then their Hangover Breakfast it to die for!

Author: Deepankar Joshi
Description: Nice ambience and some good beers . Although I really feel they could work on there appetizers . the cocktails are also not bad. What really rocks is the ambience and the music. Nice place to chill out with friends

Author: Sailesh Raja
Description:
Experienced Racial Discrimination at this Pub.
We had a table with a Fan in the terrace area which we had booked. And suddenly the waiter takes off the fan and replaces with another Fan. We assumed that fan would be working. But it turned out be not working with lame reasons told. The waiter replaces working fan to a foreigner right infront of us and says 2 mins to get the non working fan repaired. Even after 20 mins they didnt get the fan working and says "Sorry" when we are sweating out sitting over there. And adding to the point None of the waiters respond well when they see foriegners in the restaurant. Then the manager comes and says sorry for doing this purposely as the foreigner wanted the fan. I agree to the saying "Athithi Devo Bhava" but it doesnt mean you take the services of a Localite and give it to Foriegner. "It is Localites who visit the restaurant regularly."
Very Poor. Very Irresponsible. Very Bad Manners.

Author: Patricia Sen
Description: Recently after hearing about the place a lot, I decided to visit it for lunch. The restaurant is indeed a nice place with soothing ambience. A classy touch was pleasant there in every corner of the place. Coming to the food, I decided to go for the buffet which seemed really cheap to me as compared to other places. The food was really awesome with a little Turkish touch. But the major things I didn't like about the restaurant are the service and desserts. The service was really slow. Orders which were placed came almost half our late, as an instance. The desserts part was quite dull with gulab jamun. Over all it was an ok experience

Author: Shikhar Miglani
Description:
Nice place for an evening outing in Bangalore. After having a below average experience at a nearby posh restaurant, I was more than happy to visit this place.

Open till wee hours, this place boasts of a DECENT CROWD & GOOD MUSIC
We were a group of 8-9 people and it seemed that we were lucky enough to find a place at around 11ish there. :)

1) AMBIANCE: Traditional rustic interiors and wooden chairs. Rugged flooring. ALL OF THIS HAD AN ATTACHED FEEL GOOD FACTOR.
2) SERVICE: it was good. I would rate it 3.5 out of 5
3) Food & DRINKS:
a. WHEAT BEER: They brew their own beer. All of us had only wheat beer. I must say that it was really nice
b. PIZZA: We tried both vegetarian and non veg pizzas (THIN CRUST). They were NOT GOOD IN TASTE. Just just average. The non veg pizza looked so dull and was soggy in taste
c. VEG & NON Veg platters: I am sorry, I don’t remember any of the items which were served but they were much much better than the pizzas though. A platter is good enough for 2 people or even more than that if you have to stuff in drinks :)
4) MUSIC: Good music. Not very loud. Just adequate so as to provide space to the group to talk as well
5) CROWD: Good crowd. Lively faces around. Everyone in party mood
6) Pricing: Have no clue as bill was paid by others :) But it didn’t seem very expensive. Would recommend to have a look at the menu as well before you visit
7) TIMINGS: We were there till 12.30. It was still wide OPEN.
8) Overall: Go for the crowd and drinks. The food is just average. Brewed drinks are good.

Author: Rahul
Description:
The Biere Club was an intriguing name for a teetotaler me when I first heard the name 3 years ago. Back in Bangalore after higher studies, Biere Club has become a regular hangout place. I have been to this place more than half a dozen times and the most recent being yesterday.

Spread across 3 floors, this place can accommodate large crowds, but I would advise you to always book a table before you go. The ambiance is pretty cool with wood all around and good music. The music though is usually drowned in the laughter and talks of the crowd.

Coming to the food and drinks, I am a big fan of their Wheat beer. I have also tried Lager and Ale and both are decent as well. If you are in a mood for something really potent and want to get high in no time I suggest you try the long island iced beer (LIIB). I have seen many of my friends shoot up with this. Quite an interesting drink I must say. The cocktails are also pretty good and have good amounts of alcohol.

The food here is very tasty and they have quite a few options as well. I love their non veg platter, potato wedges, Pizzas, chicken drumstick and chicken popcorn. The last time we went there we tried some desserts as well. We tried the apple pie and the brownie. The brownie was really nice and the vanilla ice cream made it even better. The apple pie which I couldn't get a bite of was also very good is what my friends said.

They have a challenge for the ones who can eat and drink a lot. 12 pieces of drumstick, 2 buckets of fries, 1 pitcher beer and 1 mini baguette if you can finish in 20 mins, you don't have to pay for it. I would definitely want to try this some day.

The Biere Club has always given me good memories. Would recommend this place to anyone without second thoughts!!

Author: Vinodagouda Patil
Description:
All most same like Toit.
& its again place to have beer.
Good crowd tooo.

Author: Kranthi
Description:
excellent beer with good flavor and fresh and grilled med vegetable and pasta with cumin and dry chilli awesome well come back soon

beverage-5/5
food -5/5
ambencience-4/5
service-4/5

Author: Bishal Maskara
Description: The best place to watch an imp match fr football freaks......the ambience is vry classy.....ve been to this place 3-4 tyms bt the place nevr disappoints....definetely worth the tym and money...the platters r worth giving a try...pizza tastes amazing if available.... Overall pretty gud place to booze and hav a gala tym as well

Author: Sangya
Description:
Ambience : Has a very classy feel to it.
Menu: wheat and stout beers worth
trying. Food menu not great

Pricing: very high

Overall a place where u will feel good to be.

Author: Warren D
Description: Great beer, the wheat beer here is one to definitely try, but a crowded feel to the place and the staff seemed quite disinterested and it was hard to get drinks, starters or the staff to get what we wanted at a table on the middle level. It seemed like an alright experience but haven't ended up going back.

Author: Sudipto Saha
Description: Back to back parties.. This we chose yesterday when 13th floor was full.. Is a club or a pub.. Its a pub with brewery.. Yes dey make dere own !! ambiance less of toit.. Yes dats a benchmark for me... Beer will not make u high dissappoints.. Descent option nt a favourite!! Food portion less! Chickn wings,chikn drumsticks, 9 mugs - 3100/-

Author: Rohit Khopkar (Follow Me On Twitter @rockster999)
Description:
As we were exploring UB City yesterday; we decided to try out this micro brewery. Located right near UB City this place came highly recommended for its brews. As we entered the ambiance was that of a typical beer bar; wooden interiors with retro music playing in the back ground. The staff is very courteous and friendly; and forever happy to help. We decided to order the beer sampler first in order to decide which beer to choose from. They had five brews that day. The first was a beer infused with Indian spices; the smell was great, however the taste of the spices upon tasting was very over powering. The second was an American Rice Beer; again great to smell however lacking that certain punch in taste. The third was a coffee infused stout; bitter to the core; even a Guinness drinker will have problems swallowing that one. The fourth was an amber bock; the taste was good however we were looking for a lighter varietal. The fifth one which we eventually ordered was the Belgian Wheat beer; packed with smooth taste and a mellow wheat flavour!!! This beer was a hit for us!!! We also ordered some French fries which in my opinion are the best I have had in a long long time!!! They had a wonderful flour crust on them reminding me of the one Burger King serves abroad!!! Our bartender/waiter Murli deserves special mention as he was a true hospitality professional!!! They need to work on their beers as being a micro brewery and especially in the city of micro breweries this is what they need to work on to stand out!!! Will definitely revisit!!!

Went again post a disappointing experience at Smoke House Deli, had a wonderful time!!! The staff as always courteous and the food excellent, I had the grilled chicken burger and my wife had a fish burger (not on the menu but specially prepared for us) I also had the ale which was a mug full of bittery goodness!!! If you want great food and great service this is the place to go to!!!

Author: Pulkit Garg
Description:
Straight Pro's and Con's review

Pro's
1. The beer is very good and fresh
2. The place is always very lively
3. Super Awesome Location
4. Well done interiors with option for smoking floor.
5. Good Variety of food.

Con's
1. Food Quality is Good But the taste is not up to the mark.
2. They charge you for the beer tasting platter.
3. The prices are on the higher side.

Rest this place is worth a visit when in town.

Author: The Coffee Brewery
Description: Little expensive but worth it.. Went there with couple of friends.. We really enjoyed our time n food

Author: Sai Sharan
Description: Good beer. Very Poor ambiance my starters were served just before leaving the place. Very few starters to select from.

Author: Venky Surya
Description:
Pubbing is vivacious in Bangalore. And locals have a lot of places to choose their watering holes. It is amongst these places that freshly made brews are gaining their identity. The Biere Club on Vithal Mallya road is one such place that stamps the fresh brew concept into your head with a variety of upto 6 fresh brews on tap for you to enjoy.

In addition they serve up some mean bites and starter platters that go very well with the drinks on offer. It’s good that they write ‘platter serves a group of people’ in the menu so you know that you will get a big hearty combo of a variety of well known items on the menu.

I am told that their chicken wings in sauce are very good. Their assorted fries with the cheesy dip keeps making your fingers reach out to grab the next nibble on the bowl. But their platter takes the cake. Available in veg and non veg or a combo of veg/non veg; these are bound to satiate you and keep the conversations going as you sip on hour mugs of fresh ale, stouts and lagers.

We ordered for the falafel, hummus, pita bread, grilled aubergines, beet root dip, cheese fingers, pita bread soft and crisp variety and couscous all served up in one enormous platter. What came out was one of the best starter bites I have eaten in quite a while. The pickled olives in the platter cut any heaviness if the other accompaniments on the platter and it really went well with the lager that we had ordered.

In addition they also serve pizzas all of them 12″ with thin crust, the toppings are fresh and then cheese is still melting as the pizzas come on to your table. Their pasta though limited in variety is a good item for someone who does not want to experiment too much after a good round of drinks and starters.

Decently priced compared to pubs in Mumbai and with a unique offering of fresh brews, Biere Club seems to have hit the sweet spot with people who come to enjoy a drink after work, or a group that comes for dinner and drinks not to mention a lot of first time visitors like me who keep coming and taking back some honest opinions about wanting others to come in here and sample what they have to offer.

Unique in concept, well executed and managed and I am sure they have a following of loyal clientele to whom we are very soon likely to get appended!

Author: Suhas Padil
Description:
The Biere Club, one the many brewery pubs that are in Bangalore now. The oldest they say. Located in Lavelle road, it is easily noticeable. The place is huge and each floor has its own ambience and appeals different sections of patrons! Like you find the young and energetic crowd on the 2nd floor while a romantic set up in the 1st floor.

Alcohol I had: 1 500ml German ale. 1 mojito. 1 house specialty pomegranate vodka martini. The beer is a home brew. Fresh is what is derive from the first sip! One of the good ones out there. Mojito, one of the classics, very well made. Its fun to watch the bartender make your drink. The house specialty pomegranate vodka martini struck a chord!! Subtle yet lethally potent.

Food: I just ordered a full plate of charcoal grilled chicken skewer. Chicken was done well. It came with a spicy mustard powered sauce. Goes well with beer.

This pub has created its own identity among Bangalore. Give a visit and see how it goes.

Author: Preeti
Description: Though considered to be the premiere brew pub The Biere Club is fast losing out to competitors, and well-deserved it be. Too much of hype has meant indifferent service and discourteous waiters. That in itself is a turn off. Though the ambience is good the choice of beer is limited compared to the other microbrewery options available in Bangalore now. A good enough place to hang out with if you have nothing better to do with friends!

Author: Sridhar
Description:
Was here on a weekday during lunch time along with a friend. There was just one another table occupied.
The beer options available were lager, stout and seasonal. We started with the lager which was refreshing on a hot afternoon. Ordered some cheese garlic mushrooms. They were served almost immediately. The taste was average and not very appealing. We also ordered some assorted fries basket. The fries were fresh and good. Next we ordered the seaonal brew which was a stout flavoured with 5 spices. The beer was different though not chilled. Beer had a ting of cinnamon flavour and couldn't really make out other flavours. Disappointing part was there was no wheat beer.
Overall a good beery experience.

Author: Richa Awasthi
Description:
Good for a lazy afternoon hangout clubbed with a good music!

The place is nice, a little expensive though [What else do we expect from a place right at the heart of the town]
The ambiance makes you feel relaxed and the crowd is colorful too [Not just IT janta]. The music is loud and classy.
The food tastes ok with small servings but the liquor makes up for it :) With so many brew pubs coming up, this place still stands out with it's location, ambiance and it's taste of music. The kiwi margarita and mojito are my favorites here.

Sundays are better than Saturdays @ this place!

Author: Akshi Chilana
Description:
so it was my first time in Bangalore to a brewery , so we headed to bangaluruz much talked brewery .The Biere Club ..we went on a Saturday evening and greeted by friendly staff, being stayed in Delhi n Mumbai breweries are part of our weekend hang out , especially to taste the water of different varieties.. i enjoyed the wheat beer its stunning , light to have it...
Pitcher is a economical option when you are with bunch of besties... Pub food is my favorite.. we relished the Morocco chicken and the veg kebab platter ... DJ kept our tempo on ...music was buzzing as our gossips..2nd floor is the place to sit , thanks to the bangaluru weather breeze keeps us refreshing ..
and the icing on the cake they have Valet parking n too..
Definitely gonna be regular there :)

Author: Rahul.p.1985
Description: My favourite hang out place in Bangalore..try out the wheat beer and kebab platter is the best..expensive though

Author: Aparajitha Kannan
Description: Biere Club is a really good hang out and at a real good area might i add. They have beer, beer cocktail and dont worry the non beer folks have their choice as well. The food is also very good. I have only had the chance to try the starters and they are all delicious. i enjoyed the flaming shooter. Its almost impossible to and its one of the things i wanted to do with a friend. its the best at bier club. The 2nd floor is much chiller and more pub like which is also fun. There is a little room for all kind of people. I highly recommend.

Author: Vinod Natarajan
Description:
Wonderful place - both this and the chophouse. Lovely chilled beers, well mixed drinks, right sized portions, delicious food and courteous staff. Would defn reccomend this place for having a great time.

Come here atleast twice a month.


----- 27 May, 2013 -----

Great wheat beer.. Must try :) decent food and ambience. Very slow service though

Author: Debolina Ray (sheknowsgrub.com)
Description: One of the first of it's kind to make it in Bangalore, Biere Club definitely is the best in Bangalore as a hang out, as well as in its authenticity of brewed beers. They have a variety of pitchers for larger groups, and cocktails of beer, as well as samplers (obviously!). They do serve other alcohol, for the non beer folks(!) Usually where there's beer, there's less space for food. Thus, I have not eaten much food here, except the usual fries and wedges, but what I must recommend are the meat balls. They aren't swedish per say, but very tasty and intriguing. I love going to this place, and take my outstation friends and firang people here. Biere Club has set the bar quite high!

Author: Foodie-man | Suryad Pai
Description:
I was strolling by Lavelle road and suddenly saw this much talked about place... Decided to give it a try as I was keen to visit a microbrewary since moving to Bangalore.

Tried 5 different types if fresh brews... Ale, Lager, Wheat, Stout and Seasonal... Interestingly served in 5 test tubes

Author: Ajay Chaudhari
Description: Excellent place to drink and eat. Good music and excellent weekend crowd

Author: Shivam Gulati
Description:
The Biere club visit just makes a day or an evening happy. Prices are a little high but it is worth it.
We wanted to try something different and ordered a pitcher of beer with fruit plum.
For food we had ordered pizzas, egg sandwiches and garlic bread.
We loved the food. I would recommend a visit to this place.

Author: Neel Vora
Description: great place to hang out. just perfect brewery with their own beer. and damn good music. just love it.

Author: Kashish Goyal
Description:
Have been there numerous times. Different reasons, different days, different moods but these place has always lived upto my expectations. Comedy Nights are just amazing, but do check for the lineup at BMS prior.

You ll njoiee beer, ambiance, people n food. One of the Must visit places in Bangalore.

Author: Arjun Chakrabarti
Description:
One of the best finds in Bangalore, this lovely microbrewery will provide you with four types of freshly brewed beer (my personal favourite being the the dark ale). The Beer cocktails are also fantastic and the they go great with the mediterranean platter. The pickles that we ordered were loved by some although i personally do not enjoy sour dishes. But the two types of platters platters that we ordered were both fabulous with the dips served mexican style(with no sugar or tomato ketchup added in them, thankfully!). The final drink that we ordered was called Brain Haemmorhage Shots and they served as the perfect drink to wash down our beer (Baileys and Kahlua always act as good after dinner drinks) and provide that extra high.

The ambience is nice but with so many people it gives one the impression of the huge pubs in Germany. It is ideal to go to this brewery in a big grou and have loads of fun.

A definite recommendation from me!!!

Author: Swapnil Vyom Srivastav
Description:
IF you like brewery then The Biere Club is the place you can’t afford to miss. They have their brewery unit placed in the bar itself. They serve mainly 6 types of brewery and all of them are pretty good.
The day I went they were serving 4-5 types of breweries. My favorite brewery is wheat. All of them are good except Vanilla.
The salad, thin crust pizza and pasta’s are the show stealer in food. We ordered 2-3 types of pizza and pasta and all of them are pretty good.
The ambience of the place is pretty cool, staff is attentive.
The place is always overcrowded, we waited almost 45 mins to get seats and also a bit disappointed by the time taken to serve the food.
It’s a wonderful place to hang out, located in center of the city and severs good breweries.
Drink: 4.5/5
Food: 4.0/5
Ambience: 4.0/5
Staff: 4.0/5
Note: Always pre book the place otherwise you have to hang outside for forever. They are completely filled for entire weekends. Oder food as soon as possible.

Author: Rudra
Description: Awesome wheat beer n the seasonal oakaged ale was superb.. A place to go, when u  need fresh brewed beer in B'lr..

Author: Siddharth Satyanath
Description:
Heafty price for poor food, sometimes unappetising. Service requires some patience. The beers are alright, nothing like Toit (best) or even Arbor Brewing Company for that matter.
Live closer to Biere Club but choose to go elsewhere instead (Toit, obviously).

Author: Iris Scaife
Description:
We visited Bangalore in february and we accidentally came across the Biere club, thankfully on our first night in Bangalore and what a find it was. A Great atmosphere, great food and wonderful beer, staff are very efficient and friendly, and the place is 100 per cent clean and hygienic, we were that impressed we are having a stop of in Bangalore on our way to Goa in january just to visit this place, give it a try you will not be disappointed.

Iris Geoff scaife u.k.

Author: Rahul Raj
Description: A  microbrewery concept, try out all varieties of beers Lager, Ale, wheat, Stout  etc great food ambience overall experience great

Author: Anubhav Anand
Description: If there was zero I would give it that. Extremely poor service. Waiters just stand together near the bar. Pepperoni pizza was slightly burnt. When we wanted to tell them to exchange it there was no waiter around for like 10 mins all just standing by the bar. Too hungry to wait so ate it . Their brews are average. Definitely not worth what they're charging.

Author: Soori
Description: Great place to hangout wid friends :D had nice time....different experience fresh brewed ;)

Author: Anurag Bhatia
Description: Definitely the best pub in bangalore. The wheat beer is a must have...

Author: Sun Ray
Description:
The best beer breweries in Bang. Love d place and beers. Wheat beer is d best. Stout for hardcore kafi lovers. Starters are best order anything, it will be good. At times very crowded feel like not to enter at all. Somking n non smoking separate sections you have.

Ambiance: 4/5
Food : 4/5
Beer: 5/5
Service : 3/5
Value for Money : 4/5

Overall : 4.5/5

One of my fav n best brewery.

Author: Shilpa Thaker
Description:
One of my first night outs in Bangalore and I was but naturally recommended to go to UB city by Bangaloreans. My Mumbai friend had recommended the Biere Club and seeing it walking distance to UB city I was quite intrigued by it.

With the intention of just checking it out, we walked in, to find that there was no place. However, after a friendly chat with one of the staff members we were informed that it consisted of the first and the second floor both with different experiences. Since we were not up to the fine dining experience, we were happily recommended to go to the 2nd floor set up.
We, especially, loved the semi-open set up where we could enjoy a nice glass of Biere Sangria along with a lovely nip in the air that we Mumbaites would otherwise die for.

They have something called the Hangover Breakfast which I thought was quite innovative. There is something for everyone here. People who want to catch up over a drinks and conversations, people who enjoy the fine dining experience and people who want to catch up over some drinks and fun. Everyone is happy at the end. Full marks for their concept and execution…

For further proof, they have something called the Biere Club’s Already Infamous Biere Drinking Platter. Our order was the Biere Clubs mezze platter for 3 and the portion size was more than what we expected. The felafals were great, the tzatziki was awesome, the hummus was nice and moist, and all in all we loved to make our own wraps with the variety of dips and salads etc on the platter. We didn’t need to order for anything else as this was just perfect for 3 of us who were not very big-eaters. The music was nice and peppy and left us all in a happy state.

I don’t know if you are already there or not, but if not, please come to Mumbai soon!! (Just the way you are. No changes please)

Author: Hemalatha Kongawad
Description:
I had been wanting to visit Biere club since a long time having heard about it so much and I got to do that on a friends birthday. It was not a weekend so we did not book our table in advance. We got a table as soon as we walked in. There is nothing jazzy about the interiors, but the music being played was very nice. There are seats at the bar counter for those looking for an authentic beer bar experience.

We all ordered for the variety of beer drinks that Biere club serves - wheat, ale, lager and a cocktail beer. Do try out their cocktail beers. They really are a specialty that you might not find in many places. The crusty fried Potato Wedges tossed in hot tomato salsa are to die for. Other than this they have thin crust pizzas that are okay. They have a very extensive menu ranging from quick bites, platters, kebabs, rolls, sandwiches to pastas and pizzas. One visit is just not enough to experience all that Biere club has to offer. My reco would be to reserve a table during one of the nights they have live music(supposed to be really good) make sure you reach there on time (the place gets really crowded on weekends and during live music) and definitely try all the beer varieties they have to offer.

Author: Bharat
Description: Excellent platters, good beers. Good ambiance. Sure to visit again

Author: Pramodini Hareesha
Description: A perfect place for smokers and beer lovers...Classic pub..have their own brewery, supposedly well known for its taste!

Author: Ritesh Chopra
Description:
It was my first visit to bangalore. I went out with one of my college time friend. At first, when we entered, I was a bit apprehensive place is it appeared to be pretty small. Also, being a sat night, the club was jam packed. Finally we go a seat after 10 minutes.
We ordered 1 mug each of all of their 4-5 brewed beers available that night.. and i was pretty impressed with most of them. though none of them were strong enough. they were all mild.. we ordered a thin crust pizza and a white sauce pasta. Both were cooked to perfection.

Also, music was really good and not repetitive.

The only negative thing i found was that smoking is allowed in the normal seating area itself. So after a while it gets too smoky, specially when everyone around you is smoking( i myself smoke)...

But overall, a nice place to spend a chilled out evening :)

Author: Pavan Varada
Description:
Had been here for my b'day treat with my friends.. Have always been searching for a good microbrewery here in Bangalore after coming from Delhi... If you are a beer lover.. Then this is the place which you have to visit for sure and would gurantee that you would fall in love with the amazingly tasting beers..

N I would never forget this place cos I Won a Beer Drinking challenge with my friend and He had to paid my bill.. :) :)

Author: Tharik
Description:
I went here on a monday afternoon so I am sure I cant say much about crowd. And maybe not much about the food too because I went after lunch.
Now letsvtalk about the beer. We tried all 4 beers which were on the menu for the day.
Marzen: It was the club special and the best beer for me. it was real smooth and nice. Something you can have at any time of the day.
Lager: Not my kind of beer from a brewery. Tasted more of less like Heineken.
Wheat: Wasnt as smooth as Marzen but was pretty good. Felt more like a beer for burgers or pizza
Chilli Beer: Definitely not something I would have. Left a bad after tase in my mouth.

PS: The beer reviews were poorly based on my taste. It might change based on peoples taste.

We had the assorted fries which was basically french fries, wedges and onion rings. It was prepared well.

Author: Anupama Sundaram
Description:
The Biere club is a cool place. Looks charming both on the outside and inside. Ambience is its strong point. Brews are decent, sort of hit and miss (like their Music), some good some not so much. But then I am not much of a biere person so I don't care as much. Their wine collection could be better but hey its Biere Club so I am not crying hoax

Food is pretty decent as well and you are in luck if you get tables without reservations. The place is usually full.

Its on my list of favorites for personal reasons but I'd say its a pretty darn cool place even otherwise. I have always left in high spirits ;) All in all, a cool hangout.

Would I visit Again: why not, especially if I am around.

Would I recommend it (if you care for it?): yeah, worth a visit

Author: GC.
Description:
This place was on my wishlist for a long time and i finally went here as i wasn't allowed to enter most of the places in UB City ( read single guys with inappropriate footwear).

I tried 4 out of 5 kind of beer that they had to offer. Ale and Stout were good, though i didn't like the taste intially. Later i ordered for the Apple Ale and it ruined my palate.

So the overall experience wasn't that great. However, the starters were yummy and the quantity was more than humble.

P.S. - Go to the second floore and occupy the seating space facing the road. You'll love it.

Author: Vineet Mittal
Description: The ambience with the crowd is different from the usual places. The brew with the snacks is great. An amazing place for hangout with friends and colleagues…

Author: Dr Expat
Description:
Bangalore, I'm here to set the record straight. I'll tell you what is up to snuff and what's IN THE ROUGH.

The Biere Club is a HOITY TOITY affair opposite MG Road. It's the former place where THE BRASS would meet, and I've never been disappointed by their BREWS.

PRESCRIPTION: The pizza is pretty o-kay. Try their dark ALES and their CHICKEN STARTERS. They've got a dipping sauce that I use as an excuse for any type of food. Give me dat sauce!

VERDICT: House Call (that means check if out if you are in the neighborhood)

Author: Dilip Gopal
Description:
I had heard about this place(A new concept called microbrewery) and was excited to try this place.I have visited this place twice.I tried all the beers here.You get tasters of all the varieties for a reasonable price.The ale and the lager continue to be my favourites :).I also tried the starters- the veg platter, the moroccan chicken,wood fire thin crust pizzas and a couple of pastas which included one of my favourites-the spagheti arabiatta all of which were delicious.
The ambience,the music and the crowd are good.The only reason i have a mixed feeling is because of the differential treatment that the waiters/staff gave us on one of the occasions.I expected them to be more courteous and more responsive.We all know that the good places get crowded but that is when the service stands out.If they were courteous and attentive i would have not felt this way even if there was a delay in service.

Author: Ishant Bansal
Description: Didnt had the best of experience....bad service , bad food quality and most importantly bad drinks....better luck next time :(

Author: Jaideep Thyagaraj
Description: Nice place to chill out with like-minded friends. The variety of beers which they have got is simply overwhelming. Potato wedges here is probably the best I have had. Service is elegant. Never feel like leaving this place once you get in here. The only short-coming is the dozens of policemen who stand right there on Vittal Mallya hunting down the drunk drivers. Sigh.

Author: Dhruv Mathur
Description:
The last time we were in UB City we figured it was one of those days when one of us wasn't carrying her purse and one of us had just given us a huge treat a week back and so we were kinda low on budget for the Sky Bar kinda outing.

We walked down for 2 mins from the Louis Vuitton showroom at UB City and we reached The Biere Club.

Ambiance: Looked very attractive from the outside. Didnt take us a minute to nod and enter the place. Its a nice cute place with the smoking zone on the 1st floor. Music and decor was nice. Typical seating and surprisingly a lot of Firang crowd!

Food & Drinks:
Being a brewery, they offer Lager, Ale, wheat, Stout, Club Special and Seasonal (which is the Mango Beer). I personally am very fond of old English kinda beer so I ordered it and it was pretty satisfying! Alongside we ordered Grilled pork bangers, which were not very filling but pretty enjoyable! Was a little high to notice what my friends had ordered lol.. but anyways, it was all decent. :)

Service:
The waiters were welcoming and pleasant! The food didnt take too long either. Overall good service!

The total bill was around 800/- per person, which was pretty much worth it! Thumbs up!!

Author: SD9
Description: The Biere Club is quite packed for a Saturday evening. The downstairs area is lively and fun for an evening out with friends. The area upstairs is more along the lines of a fine dining area as against the pub area downstairs. Beautifully done, elegant and soft, perfect for a glass of Chardonnay with some buttery mushroom appetizers. Lovely place to unwind and sets quite a romantic mood as well. Must visit.

Author: Achyut Kulkarni
Description:
A great place to eat, drink and have fun with friends , parents , colleagues . It is a huge place and provides you space .
The place has more of a corporate feel due to its non-IT youth .
The food is simply superb , being a brew pub , the club makes great beer .It is almost equally crowded on every single weekday with a huge crowd on weekends.
Overall proves to be one of the very good places to be in Bangalore .

Author: Aarti Kamath
Description:
My cousin and I boiled down to this place after an extensive search about where to get drunk on a Friday evening. The reviews seemed grand about this micro brewery and after that I had higher expectations from this place after being in love with Toit!

So, this place is easy to locate, pretty close to UB City and looks great from the outside. We reached at about 5 PM on Friday and just about 2 other groups apart from us were present. The crowd started bucketing only after 6:30 PM.

In the Day light, this place is a 'lackluster' when compared to how awesome it looks from the outside. The waiters are keen on welcoming people inside; however, once you are in you need to be loud and lucid to make sure they get your attention. My opinion about their Beer is just OKAY - I would rather recommend Toit for its variety and taste. At the Biere Club, they have got about 6 main options for beer - Lager, Ale, wheat, Stout, Club Special (Indian Pale) and Seasonal (which is the Mango Beer and sadly it wasn't there).

They do have 60 ml shot-trials for which they charge you Rs.40 which is a good way to start and decide what exactly you may like. The wheat Beer was what I ordered - It looks mild in color and is smooth down the throat. It is not bitter and you may gulp more than needed. However, I found the taste a bit diluted and wasn't totally happy either. Stout is dark and absolutely bitter with a coffee flavoring. Lager is slightly bitter and tastes like most local beers. Ale is a bit bitterer than Lager and again like most bottled beers!

Pros: Looks like an English Pub from the Outside. Micro-brewery for fresh brewed beer! The crowd is good as the twilight strikes! Several expats come here due to the city proximity. Some of the staff is friendly. You can try the pizza which is about 12 inches in Diameter and is crusty too.

Cons: No Happy Hours! Seating is not very comfortable everywhere. This place wasn't as striking inside as it seemed from the outside! There was less professionalism in service for a 'pub' atmosphere. Wheat Beer is distinct however, it tastes' diluted and less laudable. Food was mediocre and cold and even after getting it 'heated' - Definitely not worth that money. The Veg platter at Rs.600 plus taxes is not any deal at that price; neither in taste nor in quantity. No Cocktails present other than their in-house Beer Cocktails. Unless you want to try out their beer, it wouldn't make sense coming to this place.

At this price and if given a choice, I would prefer Toit any day for its in-house drinks as well as food!

Recommended - if you want to hang-out amongst an interesting crowd.

Author: Rahul Rajan
Description:
Been there just twice. Valet parking in that part of town is a necessity so they have taken care of it.
Have tried only the wheat beer and I think the nachos. I'll come back with an elaborate review the next time. Weekends around 3-4pm if its raining and you'd like to sit overlooking the road, perfect place to sit back with a beer and friends.!

Author: John Nicholas
Description:
This place rocks. Amazing beer. All varieties on tap are excellent. Belgian Wit is mind blowing. Dark Ale is one of the best Ales I ever had. Mango beer is refreshing and addictive. My friends had Lager and Stout and praised them a lot. Will try them next time.

Ambiance is good. Liked the wall painting. Something different. Food is average but expensive.
Service is slow but acceptable.

Author: Sumeet Puj
Description: Ultimate mind boggling wheat beer!! Loved the place the ambience n the wheat beer is damn damn good I'm in love with the beer

Author: Nisha
Description:
Visited this place on 14 April.

The music is very loud (& the waiter couldn't assist in getting the volume levels down) and the chairs were uncomfortable.

Tried the wheat beer. It was fairly nice. Not too sure if is reasonably priced; but hey for a handcrafted brewery to be located within the city is a blessing & comparable to the ones in other cities. Unlike Delhi & Mumbai where one has to go to Gurgaon & Thane respectively to try out such beers.

Compared to Arbor brewing company near MG road, this place lacked in the ambience and variety department.

Author: Pint
Description: Loved this place - I forget the name but they had a delicious locally brewed wheat beer that I fell in love with. The guy pouring us the beer was super quick with all sorts of tips and info as well. Too bad they are so far away from where I live - I'd have liked to be back often.

Author: Navin Singh
Description: Beers are very tasty and food is decent. However beers may not appeal to all as they are of different flavours and styles that some may not like. Prices are rather high however for what you get.

Author: Dechen
Description: Being an avid beer lover & having tried different sorts of beer from all over. Biere club is a must visit in bangalore. Service is not superb. However the beer is good, u shud try all. The oven pizzas are to die for

Author: Chris Christoforou
Description:
Some really good beers! They do a really nice selection of beers from lagers to wheat beers and stouts (light and dark)
On the food side they supply very nice platters which come on massive serving plates so you can easily fill yourself up on these platters alone.
It as shame there is no lift you have to walk up the stairs to get to the nice bit of the place which is right at the top where you can sit semi outside!

All in all, if you want good beer and drinks with a nice atmosphere then this is the place to go in lavelle road area, certainly better than other places in UB city

Author: Deepika
Description: The biere club! Located in the center of Lavellel road, grabbing all the attention it wants. The place is fine, the wooden texture and the home made beer really sets the mood. Little expensive but its not something you wouldn't be prepared for. The placed serves real good pizzas and pasta. The various beers are also good. The service is little slow. This place is huge, has three floors, the top most is the noisiest one and also offers a smoking zone.

Author: Allen Mathias
Description:
Rats. After enjoying a few brews on the second floor we decided to give the calmer first floor a try. The brew cones are visible here and so were some rats who were enjoying some of the fallen brews in these chambers. On alerting the staff their reactions indicated prior knowledge and the response indicated sheer disregard of the issue.
While all of us acknowledge the fact that hygiene is the lowest priority even in the best restaurants in our country we at least expect certain things to remain behind the curtains.

Lets hope the establishment here has got the gumption to set new standards in hygiene as it has in brews. Wishful thinking? I hope not!

This is my third edit on this post. Heck I really like this place and hope that this gets fixed. This was one of the first micro breweries in town and I would hate to have to stop going here.

Author: Ami R
Description: I love the beer club. the club, the atmosphere,ambience and above all the beer. their regular beers are great but we tried the mango beer once which was horrible. the food is also decent. we had pizzas and fries and sandwiches....all of which we enjoyed. however the only problem we faced a couple of times was when we booked they always said they didn't have a booking in our name which is pretty poor if you have booked a table for birthday celebrations. be sure to get the persons name who takes your booking.

Author: Bhook Laagli
Description:
The next obvious choice when you watch the Gayle storm live in Chinnaswamy stadium is to head to Biere Club to celebrate RCB's win. Went there with three more people after the match. Found it empty for a change and managed to get a table promptly.
Ordered LIIB's for the friends (Long Island Ice Beer). The first set of LIIBs had too much lime in it and did not enjoy it. However, the second round of LIIBs was perfectly done. Also since I don't drink I went for my all time favorite - Hot and Spicy Mary. It failed to live up to my expectations this time as did not find the right measure of tabasco and tomato juice.
For starters we had Crusty Fried Potato Wedges tossed in Hot Tomato Salsa and Moroccan Spiced Chicken. The Moroccan Spiced Chicken was done up very well and also the dips that they gave with it were yummy. One was a mayonnaise based dip and the other a chili based so loved the whole combination. However the potato wedges were neither crusty nor the thin almost un-existing salsa sauce failed to satisfy my taste buds.
For mains we had a Pasta with Fresh Tomato and Basil Sauce which was done fairly well but would not suit everyone's palette.
