What has shocked me is The Biere Club does not give ketchup with any of its orders. Surely they should understand that Indians like ketchup with their food and it can be disappointing for many people to be denied ketchup.
In a nutshell, not one of the best experiences I have had at Biere Club.
Final verdict-
Ambiance- 4.5 on 5
Service- 4 on 5
Food- 3.5 on 5
Drinks- 3 on 5

Author: Kiran
Description:
The Place- Nothing much about the ambiance it is pleasant, has 3 floors , cozy is what describes it best.

Accessibility -Located on lavelle road quite easy to get to , very near to Richmond circle, plenty of bus and rickshaw which is quite helpful for those of us who Do not drink and drive

Music - Not that great , I could hardly hear what they were playing but it is not necessarily a bad thing can have good conversation without having to shout.

Beer-

Wheat beer- I have tried their wheat beer and it is spot on very similar to what was served in Doolaly Pune, great aroma and great taste - a very full bodied drink and not bitter. Disappointed, it was not on tap on my last visit.

Stout- A dark slightly bitter drink.

Indian Pale Ale- Slightly similar to the wheat beer but a little bitter.

Snackers-

Pizza - A good option if you visit in a group , was well made and tasty
Platter - Had gone for the veg version , it was ok
French fries/finger chips- A good tit bit plenty in quantity, would have preferred if the dip had a bit of spice in it.

A must visit place for all beer fans.

Author: Priyanka Daga
Description: If I have folks in to show around Bangalore Biere Club is on the list, makes me happy we have such spots to hang out in Bangalore. Of course its a perfect gastropub. Good food with brewed beer. Must go there.

Author: Sanchita Dasgupta
Description: They screen all the matches and they brew their own beer. What more can one want? The staff is friendly and the food is good, though everything takes a little longer than it should to come to the table. that could be because they are crowded most of the time. Quite a popular place with the people who lead the UB City kind of lifestyle and like watching matches at pubs.

Author: Karan Kapur
Description:
The wheat beer and the Indian ale(club special) are my favourites.The wheat beer is very smooth,frothy and goes down very well,while the Indian ale is a little bitter and much stronger than the wheat.My strong advice is to follow a pitcher of wheat beer with a pitcher of the Indian ale.Accompany it with a seafood platter.Avoid the steaks on the first level.
The place is pricy,but definitely worth it.

Author: Shreya Sinha
Description:
Best place if you are fan of wheat beer. Cozy sitting and great theme inside allows spending lazy hours for long. Their food menu is quite short but starters are good. The sitting are is the best part of it.


Ambiance - 8/10
Food quality - 8/10
Cost - 7/10
Service - 7/10

Author: Arjun Krishna
Description:
Lived in Bangalore for 3 years, hadn't been to Biere Club - had to remedy that ASAP and remedy that we did.

The visit started off on a sour note though, the usual reservation mix up led to us having to wait a good 20 minutes before being seated. Once seated though the evening was off to a great start with the beer flowing free and indigo fm playing lightly on their speakers.
The place was crowded and pleasantly boisterous - not too loud, not too quiet - just right! :)

Our trio ended up having a pint each of the Wheat, the lager, the ale and the Seasonal. The Wheat and the ale stood out from the rest but i guess this is entirely a matter of personal preference - do experiment and try everything so you know what you like - to this end, as expected they do offer samplers at 40 bucks a pop (they arrive in really tall shot glasses that one may mistake for light fixtures!)
I hear the Shandy (Beer+Sprite) and the cocktails are also pretty impressive - alas that'll hafta wait until next time.

The Appetizers -
The Grilled Bacon wrapped sausages with Salsa Verde satisfied our carnal yearnings while the Crispy fried Cheesy Garlic mushrooms left us wanting more!
We also had the non veg kebab platter which was pretty impressive but makes a crater on your wallet as expected - at over 800 bucks.
Friends' had spoken highly of the Breakfast pizza here with a runny egg on top - but this was not be, the waiter gave us a quizzical look when we asked for this - Feeling adventurous we decided to try the Seafood pizza instead which came with Shrimp, Squid and anchovies - Would not recommend! It lacked balance and the flavor was completely off.

I will most definitely go back. Must.complete.my.research!

Author: Sanjay Sukumar
Description:
Let me begin with the service,sloppy and unorganized.
I came here for the hangover breakfast special.
The chocolate milkshake was Hershey's syrup with diluted milk and ice.
The pancakes were raw and uncooked(uncooked batter stuck on the fork).
The portions were small for the fine dine price were paying.
The frittatas were bland and the scrambled eggs off mediocre portions.
The bread(hot buttered crusty bread) was hard and chewy.
The bacon was a little too crispy.
Overall,Koshys remains no.1 for a good English/American breakfast.

Author: Hungry Panda
Description: superb place to try the many varieties of Beer... the food is good too but a little too pricey for my pocket :D... nice crowd and good location.. would be nice to have some offers though..

Author: Chakradar Raju
Description: Never thought beer could get me high till i tried wheat beer at this place

Author: Madhurima Garodia Saraf
Description:
a good place to hang out...located near UB city n with the kinda crowd that comes...it looks very expensive but its actually very reasonable
the service is good
and so is the ambience
the food dere is also good..
its always crowded and they have 2 floors as well
overall a good place to catch up with friends

Author: Aaron Deryl
Description:
This place is THE place for beer lovers!! This is my first time at an actual brewery... All my life I've just chilled over the regulars,I mean the fosters, Budweisers and not to forget the ubiquitous Kingfishers... Alright so this was a totally new n unique experience for me...
The Biere Club looked classy n cool at the same time... From the moment I stepped in I knew it had a great fan following,even though the place was established just about two years back. Classic rock n country music were playing,n these are of course ingredients to the art of chilling in style. The waiters were polite n spoke very good English. This is one of the problems in many of our diners where it's kinda tough to actually understand what their version about an item on the menu is. Here the waiter explained to us perfectly about the varieties of beer available. We decided to give lager beer a skip for next time. We tried ale,stout and wheat. Ale had a sweetish aftersweet n would be the perfect choice for someone who isn't fond of the hard taste of beer. . Wheat was on a medium. But stout.. It was a whole new level for me. It was dark coloured n was actually reminding me of black coffee. It was such a solace to have pure beer which was a relief from the usual annoying "fillers". One pint would leave a good feeling n the second one brought in the slight buzz. We ordered a platter for three which tasted good but it was just okay since my taste buds have definitely been pampered better. It was the thin crust pizza that really impressed me. It proved to me again that the routine pizzas we order are just a mix up of massive crust n unhealthy levels of cheese with very little meat. For the same price this one had good quantities of meat and was extremely well made. Totally loved this one.
Another specialty of the Biere club is that it holds events n the event for that night was a stand up comedy show. It was indeed funny n gave us a few good laughs that were much needed after a long n tiring day. The beer the sumptuous food and the fun event. Completely made the evening!!
I'm definitely comin back to check out more types of beers as well as this exquisite menu that's filled with choices for the foodie,all served by well behaved n courteous waiters who can point out exactly what we are looking for,on the menu. Totally justifies a 4.5 rating from me!! Cheers!!

Author: Murali
Description:
Bier Lovaah ?? You will enjoy 'Biere Club' to the max :)

They brew 8 varieties of beer, including 'mango beer', a seasonal brew. You can try them before you place the order. I've almost tried all the brews, they were good (am not a beer lover by the way). The best time to visit this place is the summer.

The Mediterranean food served here is excellent (You will only enjoy it if you are a food-explorer. Not many enjoy this cuisine).

The ambiance was fantastic. They play a Very Good Music :)

Food: 3.75 / 5
Ambiance: 4 / 5
Service: 3.75 / 5
Looks: 4 / 5

The Feel: 4 / 5

'Love the Bier'
'Leisure"
A 'Must Visit' if you are a Food-Explorer.

Author: Mithila
Description:
Biere club is located in the heart of Bangalore..!! This hot spot is one place the party animals would not want to miss..beer served here are mind blowing n the best part is they provide free beer samples ....!!
Staffs are pretty friendly
Cheers ...

Author: Aniruddha Mukherjee
Description: Genuine brews! Still not Brooklyn Lager, but will do (thus spake my snotty self!). Good place to hand around with people with expensive taste. There is a lot to choose from on the menu.

Author: Arvind
Description: The Biere Club is perfectly located near UB city. I would give a 5 star for the ambience. They had some four varieties of brewed beers and all tasted good. Better to try the beer before ordering - They serve beer in test tube for tasting.  The place is quite happening right from the music they play, the wall decors, etc., The prices are bit on the higher side but defintely worth the money. The place is heavily crowded during weekends, so better go early to secure a good spot.

Author: Sajal Singh
Description: great place to hang out with friends esp. if ur a Beer lover... food is decent... service efficient..really enjoyed it...

Author: Shriram R
Description:
Here's what you will go through if you ever visit this place (I suggest you might as well spend your money somewhere else) -
1. Once you go in, if your stars are favoring you, you will manage to grab the attention of the waiters otherwise might as well decide the order and convey the same to the chef / kitchen.
2. This is such a pompous place where the waiters think they are doing a favor by listening to the customer's orders.
3. Also they don't know what menu to display when - when I went here Pizzas / Pastas weren't available but apparently the waiter did not want us to know that. It was only once we asked were we told "hey you village folks this menu ain't available so go take a hike".
4. The tables aren't set up with cutlery so once the food comes its when the waiters realize "Hey how are the customers supposed to eat?" they give the cutlery (You could request them but hey good luck catching their attention). The fork comes first and then after an inordinate wait of 10 minutes comes the knife and spoon (Sorry we weren't offered any spoons because they did not think we required spoons).
5. If you are there - please drink the beer and come out. The food is beyond pathetic - one of my friends was fed an undercooked omelette which he had to vomit because of the same reason because it was undercooked.
6. The final straw - them waiters dont know what is a feedback form. When we asked for one we were given a birthday details card. How presumptuous of that waiter to assume "hey we love this place". are you uneducated or simply unmotivated to work on a saturday. If no please don't wait on my table - go take a hike somewhere else.
7. FYI to all future customers - when we asked for a plain glass we were given a stained old unwashed glass and sorry they serve beer but not water (apparently they don't want to).

End word - Pompous, presumptuous, bad food and extremely disgusting and repulsive waiting / ordering experience. What a colossal waste of money, energy.

Thanks but no thanks Biere Club : your restaurant experience is like getting kicked oneself shot twice in the head and still not dying and living to tell the tale.


Shriram

Author: Akilesh Sood
Description: Thank you Beer Gods for Bieri Club. No nonsense no frills brew pub that will keep you coming back for more. Love the convenience of this place.

Author: Anumit Sasidharan
Description: Definitely worth a visit if you love beer. They have a nice variety of beers. Ones of note during our visit were the Belgian Strong Ale, Hefeweizen and Abbey Ale. Both the vegetarian and non-vegetarian platters offer enough variety if not quantity, and are worth it. Unfortunately, the service was painfully slow. Also, the restrooms are inconveniently located on the first floor. The ambiance, while better than the usual fare, could be even better.  But on the whole, the great brews and the nice location close to UB city make it a worthwhile place to visit when you're around the area.

Author: Parul
Description: It is what it says it is. A good Beer/Brew Club, with inhouse brewery and perfect Ambiance to match various ales. Food menu is a little restrictive, but you don't really go there to eat!

Author: Subham Ghosh
Description:
The Ultimate place for 'Beer' lovers..i was new to bangalore and when i first visited this place ,it suprised the hell out of me. Starting from the ambience, the food quality and the crowd its a good party place. Not to forget about the beer they serve...awesome every single penny is worth it if u get that kinda taste.
If u want to hangout with ur friends ,sit and have a nyc chat while great quality of beer and food is served to you.
This is THE PLACE to be.
Cheers and happy Drinking..:)

Author: Om Vikram Thapa
Description: Hav been here searchin for breweries. Had pizza and tasted almost all the varieties (they provide samples) i guess 2nd floor is more happenin than ground floor. Sundays brunch wid fresh beer is well known.. But i will go wid TOIT anyday if compared

Author: Machri
Description: I think the biere club is a bit overrated.. Service was disappointing and the food was just mediocre..the beer is fine but you do have a lot of other options in bangalore with their own breweries.

Author: Mild Seven
Description: a decent place to be...however the beers are almost same...didn't appreciate much as I felt it was quiet conjusted

Author: Ekta Prasad
Description:
Upside - A well designed place..good furniture,nice graphiti etc. The pizzas are great and so is the Beer.
Downside - Have been turned down a couple of times for lack of Beer Cocktail. It is "never" available & they may as well strike it off their Menu.
The service is slow. Air conditioning is poor in certain areas.

Author: Shruti
Description: Nice place to sit and chat ,, i like 4 cheese pizza there , with wheat beer...

Author: Mrs. M Roy
Description: My rating is for the beer, talking positives the brew acts as d magnet though mostly wheat gets over by 9 r half past 9.The smoked chicken pizza joined hands wid my drink. The lady @ the reception is not worth her work. The first tyme I went dere was very disappointd wid gettin a place even stand 2 near the servings. Newaz kuddoz 2 d beer & bookinz must n d onset of weekend.cheerz \m/

Author: Priyanka
Description:
I went there for lunch with my brother few months back.. Both of us are not beer fans hence we preferred the 1st floor which is a proper dinning room. We had a mushroom stater which was good. For a cheese lover it's a great dish because it was mushroom baked in cheese.. we ordered the soup of the day which was tomato and basal soup whic was amazing..Main course we picked a chicken dish but found a hair in it. The staff was extremely quick in replacing it infact they were ok with us changing d dish too. Hence ordered a mushroom burger which was amazing...but the best dish was the toffee pudding which was the desert. it just had a great flavor and a really delightful sponge cake. whenever i think of this place i think of that delicious toffee pudding. will definitely go back here just for the desert :)

Overall a good experience!!

Author: Kalika Bali
Description:
The Bier or Beer is by far the best in Bangalore. I have been here twice and tried the Indian Pale Ale and the Belgian Witbier. Both are excellent and considering what one has to pay for a small bottle of Hoegaarden in some of the other places around town, excellent value for money. I am not a beer drinker normally as am not a fan of Indian lager. But having lived in Belgium in the past can really appreciate good beer when I can get it.
Can't say much about the food. We only tried some Moroccan Chicken Kebabs which were excellent, just the right amount of spice and tender. Am looking forward to trying more food and beer from their microbreweries.

Author: Mohammad Farhan
Description:
The Biere Club scores over all the other pubs in Bangalore for location and ambiance. Its near the UB City and attracts the best crowd of Bangalore. Please do reserve your seats prior to your visit else you would be seen arranging for chairs.
The environment is bustling and they really play some good music(numbers you would prefer while you drink). Now I dont drink but I can assure you that this place offers "some best beer"(quoting my friends who had drinks).
The place is very expensive and hence no prizes for guessing that it attracts a lot of foreigners. Its not a place where you would go for just catching up a drink with your friends. It can be good options for people looking to have drinks with their foreigner friends/clients.
The staff is really busy and the service is just OK.

Author: Kiran Narayan
Description:
Decent Stout served at this place, good for evening gathering with office colleagues or Friday night with Friends. Delicious apetizers and one place that actually has fresh Mozarella in their bruschetta.

You will have a nice evening.

Author: Sam Wals
Description:
Service
Once the order is placed, it will be there at your table before you know it. The music and the crowd is simply too good, so you won't actually mind the delay in placing the order.

Drinks
The beer is really fresh, true to its name and nature. And it goes so well with the music. I personally loved the german lager. But the club special is the one if you like a strong aroma. And stout is the one which can get you high.

Food
There are many platters to choose from. Everything in the menu goes really well with the beer. The place is all about beer after all. The food is proper American style, dont expect it to be spicy.

There is an option for fine dining. I would suggest this pub any day. You will fall in love with the place as soon as you enter it.

Author: R Bhaskar Chandra
Description:
No comments on food, Good Service, Really good ambiance, Expensive

Been here quite a few times and best way to start is to have the sample set. Pick your taste and enjoy.
I always order the same items and have a great time at this place.

Setting : Friends + Dates

Author: Tanya Sharma
Description:
Biere Club is a charming place and has become a landmark in Bangalore in a matter of two years. They are also easily the pioneers of micro-breweries in the city.

It's crowded whenever I go; people love the beer here and I find myself a big fan of their wheat beer. They have some decent varieties, and generate some interesting special beers every week. I tried the Belgium Yale once. Smooth and bursting with the kicks!
The music is good. They have a different ambiance for every one of their three floors. I really liked their wall art - it's so French! Of course, the second floor is where it's all happening. The best of the city's hip crowd are chilling here with their mugs of beer, striking a conversation with strangers, celebrating with neighbouring tables - - original pub culture, this is !!

The downside is that the food is just ok. I think I've tried every veg starter on the menu and I haven't been too impressed with any of them. And the service could be better because we've been charged for extra beer and food 4 times already!
Nevertheless, it draws in a super crowd. Super time, super beer! If you haven't been there yet, you're really missing something!

Author: Mohammad Danish
Description: Good hangout place for the youth, service is excellent with amazing crowd. I loved the place and the recommended item would be their Platters, definitely recommended :)

Author: Puja Trivedi
Description:
The bier club..a nice pub located near UB city...The moment you will enter the pub,you will start feeling good about you,about the pub and about everything around.It has a good feel in it.
The music they play is awesome..the pictures on the wall add to its charm.
There are micro breweries which brew beer and they serve fresh beer..the taste of beer is also different and nice
The Foodliquor menu has a wide variety of options with food items you will hardly find anywhere else in Bangalore.
Downside-
1)Its full during most of the weekends.So make prior booking or be ready to stand near the bar.
2)Wheat beer is not available more often than not.

Author: Shakir Mohaseen
Description:
Been to Biere club a number of times and have never been disappointed. Their self brewed beers are really good. The place has 3 floors in total. The ground floor which is very much like an old pub with nice bar stools and spacious table arrangements. The 1st floor is now called the Chophouse specializing in Steak. The 2nd floor is the open rooftop where you can smoke and enjoy your drinks.

I always go for the Weiss beer which is the strongest of the lot and its good. Their snacks like the potato wedges are real good. The have an option of cooking the wedges with different variety of sauces which is a great hit among me and my friends. The pizza are really yum as well. The chicken wings are a big hit among us as well.

Author: Ashwini Kalapala
Description: This place easily serves one of the best beers in the city.  I tried the nonveg platter along with it and it was heavenly. It has really awesome food and trust me its goes great with great beer. Must try place for beer and food lovers..

Author: Sumana Srivastava
Description:
This was the first microbrewery I had visited and I was quite disappointed with the beer. Not much differentiation between the beers in terms of flavors etc. But the rest of the bar menu was good and made up for it.

But whats worth mentioning is the food menu both in terms of quantity and quality it was an amazing feast.

Would recommend for a Sunday afternoon chilling...the ambiance is good and when the place fills up it gives a old world charm with its wooden flooring and decor. Loved the overall experience.

Author: Shiba
Description: Most amazing place for Beer lovers in Bangalore. They serve Best beers in the city, and with amazing food...... Manager and employees are really good and helpful, though last time, I felt one or two of them were not behaving properly (to someone sitting next to my table), may be they were new to Biere Club, and need time to adapt to the Club...... Love the way, you guys have divided all three floors, with different ambience. But, last time when i went to Biere Club, was little disappointed to see that 1st floor was converted into Fine Dining, ealier it was better...... I so miss Biere Club here in Mumbai.....  Keep up the good work guys !

Author: Vipul Mittal
Description: This place is perfect in terms of variety of food, beer and the ambience. Just go there and chill. The beers brewed there are really good and offer a good alternative to the regular stuff available elsewhere.

Author: Abhishek Jaiswal
Description:
No Matter you are here on a Saturday Afternoon or a Monday evening, you will find this place flooded with beer lovers loitering around the place with a mug of fresh beer in their hand and grooving to the music.

This Place serves the best beer in the city. Not to forget the good food they serve.

If you are in Bangalore..this place is a MUST VISIT !!

Author: BinuD
Description: The food is great, the beer is good third best beer in town however the best food of all the breweries.

Author: Shubhra Dixit
Description:
I loved the Mario Miranda style on the extreme right wall of the ground floor. I suspect it may be a creation of Paul Fernandes. The black sofas are super-comfortable. Like another brewery in the city, their samplers come at a cost! That, I found a little surprising. A suggestion - make the test tube a smaller shot glass and provide a free sample to each table. Goodwill takes you a longer way than does immediate profit!

Anyhow, the German Pilsner was the tastiest one to me and we couldn't resist calling for it mug after mug! On the other hand, I couldn't get myself to like the burnt and strong after taste of the Stout and the Indian Ale. (I do hope I am getting the names right).

The bacon wrapped sausages tasted good because of an interesting paste of capers on them. Though I must give it up to The Biere Club for amazing beer, I do have a couple of food-related regrets to share! What disappointed me in general was the lack of flexibility. For me, being treated like your opinion matters is a big plus for any restaurant. We love meat - but it's nice to see a few olives or some mushrooms or a couple of strips of peppers on your pizza along with some meat - makes it colorful as well as flavored. Since we couldn't find any such pizza on the menu, we requested our pepperoni, ham and cherizo pizza to have any one veggie. But even on suggesting paying additionally for toppings, we couldn't get them to put some veggies on our pizza. So we went along with how it was described on the menu. And what a shame - the pizza was far from delicious. The 'thin crust' had transformed to a dry crust and the meat had no taste. Maybe we made the mistake of eating at an Italian restaurant the day before - Toscano, who know how to get their pizza right - and perhaps making an unfair comparison.

Their menu from the Beer Chop House on the first floor looks far more promising and sinful than does the in-house one. But The Biere Club refuses to serve steaks and other main course dishes downstairs. This may be a ploy to get people to walk into the Chop House which was absolutely vacant on an otherwise busy Sunday afternoon.

A few highs, and some lows - The Biere Club failed my expectations. Let's not blame the expectations.

Author: Saurabh Srivastava
Description:
Its really about beer - all beer I have had over the last few months have been good. Recommend try the sampler first and then pick the right one if you plan to have 2-3 beers in the evening. The starter platter is excellent and quite filling especially for a long evening with > 5 drinks.
The meats are really fine and found them excellent with ales, stouts and other darker beers.
Atmosphere is good, service fast and great ambience. Walls painted like you will see at Leopold in Mumbai.

Author: Vinu S
Description: Food is good however the service was very delayed. Went there with my friends and had to wait half an hour each order.

Author: Suresh Babu
Description: From the UK, so I do enjoy a good pint. that being said, its rare to find a microbrewery that has anything that tastes remotely better than a watered down blonde ale. prices are UK prices, but the house beer was better than most. Watch out for your bill to get out of control if you go with a group. will be back

Author: Foodieonadiet
Description:
This is one happening place on lavelle road...
beautifully made.. and it is packed everytime you think of going there.. so better book well in advance..
go there if ur a beer lover and wanna experiment on various flavours!!
the food is nice too..
A must visiti!!

Author: Mezza
Description:
The beer whatever you want is never there ,the food is tasty however took nearly 45 mins to get to us . maybe all brewerys have the same problem. the staff like the other brewery is arrogant as well

Value: 5/10
Food : 8/10
Ambience : 4/10
Service: 3/10

Author: Karthik Shetty
Description:
Biere Club opened up as the city's first microbrewery. Located in a part of town that's known to be the center stage for all the Page 3 and the usual hoity-toity types, The Biere Club is a class above the other micro breweries in town at this point in time. While Toit (the nearest competition) caters to a different age segment, The Biere Club is a classier act. Done up like a typical kicked back place with seating across 2 floor (with their newly opened restaurant Chop House in between), they also have a graffiti wall, complete with Mario Miranda cartoons. The atmosphere is completely laid back, and there's a sense of calm even in the chaotic madness that can ensue, especially during weekends. If pubs are supposed to have acoustics such that there is a lot of buzz when only a few tables are occupied, then this place gets full marks.

The micro-brewery boasts of multiple kinds of beers - one lager, three ales, and a host of speciality ales/lagers that the brewmaster may create. If you want your head to start buzzing, then one pint of the dark ale can get you there. The dark ale is really quite superb, and no other micro brewery has got the dark ale anywhere close to theirs.

The starters, at least most of the, seem to have a Mediterranean tilt to them. The flavours are such that they aren't very spicy, but they're still pretty darn good. The chicken wings are really good, and the spicy dip along with it is perfect when you're downing beers. The bacon wrapped sausages with salsa are awesome... I mean bacon is superb as it is, and anything with bacon will naturally be good. We also loved the sea food platter, with the batter fried beauties being done to near perfection.

The Moroccan spiced chicken and the Arabian lamb keftas are also really good - the Moroccan chicken being quite superb actually. If you're the kind who likes munching on chicken off a bone, the cilantro flavoured drumsticks are the way to go. The crispy fried garlic mushrooms are a good option for vegetarians, the garlic and cheese combining well to impart decent amount of flavour to the mushrooms. The thin crust pizzas are quite a welcome sight on the menu, and are fairly decent if you aren't a finicky gourmet diner.

All the dishes are very colourfully plated, and are visually quite appealing. Once you're done with the starters and aren't slurring after your drinks, then if you are planning on have 'main course', then, well, you could try the risottos and pastas that are pretty decent. But if you wanna stick to the whole pub atmosphere, then you should try the sandwiches, or even the pork bangers and mash, which really is quite good. I've never stayed for dessert, so I wouldn't know how they fare on that front.

The service at the Biere Club is quite good and prompt. The staff is well informed (relative, judge for yourself) and the service time is fairly quick, and even on crowded days, they usually manage to deliver without too much of a delay. The staff is also quite alert, and are usually on the spot to fill glasses or ask for refills. Overall, Biere Club delivers on most fronts, and for regulars, the only wish is that all types of lagers are available at any given time.

Author: Dozo
Description: A meat place that does meat well. Good beer. Vegetarians will feel let down here. And apart from the meat and beer, the chi-chi atmosphere and people-watching are basically the other attractions.

Author: Vivek Foodie
Description: Good place and good food however the portion size could be a little bigger for the price they charge. The place was full and service was a bit slow, they should hire more staff.

Author: Rohan Khanduja
Description:
The beer is just great at this place..

The decor of this place has graffiti on its walls and the place is tightly packed all the time and I always end up standing and drinking.
I think i should start thinking about reserving a place here.
They have a smoking section which is on the 1st floor. I didnt get a a chance to sit there but i am sure it would be equally good at the top as well.

Many times you end up seeing a lot of famous page 3 personalities here partying on a saturday night.
I have been lucky at that.

A well deserved place when you wanna eat and drink beer.
They serve different varieties of beer and ensure that you try them out in small glasses which you could get if you wish to try them out before ordering. They have different styled beer from the darkest to the lightest in shades and flavours.

To make it simple order the Wheat or Ale to drink something with a good taste.

Over food my recommendation would be to just order any platter as it has most of all the content what is there in the menu apart from the Omelettes.

Do not miss out on the different Beer cocktails if you wanna drink rigorously. Do not worry a lot of cabs are always standing outside this place to ensure you reach home safely.
This place lies on Lavelle Road and Vittal Malya Road junction which makes it a perfect to locate. Since this property is very close to UB City which attracts a lot of customers to walk in to this place

It happened to be always evenings when I visited this place and I always found it to be among the best for drinking beer. :)

Cheers !
Give this place a chance if you love beer.

Author: Varun S
Description:
Beers and Pizzas. Though these two words aren't enough to describe this place, still they make the most of what this place is. We went in late despite reservation and after a bit of argument with the manager happened to the get the seat.

Knew this place was famous for beers and ordered ale. My oh my, it was a great delight, guzzled a litre in no time. Raked in some other stuff from my friend's mugs too

German Pilsner: Decent
Wheat-beer: Good
Ale: Awesome

Pizzas we ordered:
1) Sun-dried, mushroom, tomato and goat-cheese - Awesome, awesome pizza, tasted just perfect with the beer and otherwise too
2) Charred sweet peppers and smoked cheese - a bit of let down, but okay

Ambiance - 7/10 (Nice pub ambiance)
Food and beverages- 8/10 (good)
Service - 8/10 (good, despite the fact that place was full of people)
Wallet Factor - 8/10 (reasonable)
Overall - 8/10

Author: Harsha R
Description: Very friendly staff... Good beer selection too. My friend and I once went when there was a stand-up comedy show (coincidence). We just wanted to catch up and have beer and not really interested in any shows. We were allowed but only at the bar counter... No tables, so it was not really comfortable. We had one round of beer and went to some other place. So please call to find out and book a table is what I can suggest.

Author: Sindhu
Description: Love the high-backed sofa-chairs on the top floor (what are they called?). Perfect to unwind on friday evenings with a small group. Big fan of the cocktails and the drinking platters. The place to celebrate match victories with your team and cheering squad. :)

Author: Agratha Dinakaran
Description:
Biere Club is one of my favourite 'beer' hangouts in the city. I usually am loyal to my Wheat Biere here and it's almost always a favourite for most people I know - the ones who prefer their beer light, minus the bitter taste. The Stout Biere here is another gem.

I'm usually not the biggest fan of the food here. I think their Hummus and Falafel and some other Mediterranean food is alright, but their platters and other bites don't come off as particular 'tasty' food. It's okay and there is some flavour but it lacks zing. It's not something I particularly enjoy paying for.

Which is why when they opened their Chophouse, I was mighty excited. I like good food with my alcohol and it was about time Biere Club stepped it up for me. The menu was extensive and honestly, very impressive. With a meatier menu in place, I placed an order for a Chicken Caesar Salad and a Duck Bruschetta. Much to my chagrin, the server conveniently forgot to add chicken to my salad and the dressing was bland. The bruschetta with shredded duck was actually quite nice, but the quantity was abysmally disappointing (just ONE slice of bread). The complimentary bread basket served rusk, not bread, in my opinion.

For our mains, I ordered the Pork Chops and my friend ordered the Lamb Shanks. The lamb meat was quite soft, but was doused in a very salty marinade that we could not deal with. My pork chops came in a good sauce, along with caramelized apples, but the meat was extremely tough to cut through. I sent it back to the kitchen, and they said they'll replace it with fresh pork chops done to perfection. However, the dish was returned to me in the exact same way I had sent it.

Overall, I was unhappy with my dinner experience. Maybe this was just an off-day for them, but it was enough for me to not go back there again for a meal. Especially, when the place is a little pricey and there is a good chance that the food might disappoint again. My advice: Stick to their beers and you're good. The beer and the service has earned a 3-star rating from me.

Author: Pritham
Description:
This is the best place for having the In House brew style beer (microbrewery), love the Stout, Wheat and Dark Ale. A must visit for people who love to try different types and flavour of beer. Try their new Chilli Lager its different and great.

Also there are many restaurants trying to open something similar, but let me tell you one thing they are NO WHERE CLOSE!!

Biere Club stands out food wise too, they have excellent presentation and the food is absolutely finger licking good. I simply cannot get enough of this place. Service may be slow at times but its understandable.

Highly Recommended:

* Olive Dash, Bacon wrapped sausages (blissful), Assorted Crostini, Salads are great and so are the Potato Wedges perfectly crispy on the outside and melts like butter on the inside.
* Thin Crust Pitza's; personal favorites; Grilled Mediterranean Veg & basil pesto and the one with Sundried Tomato's and Fetta.
* The Mini Lamb Kibbeh burgers served with relish is good too. Their Non-Vegetarian platter is excellent as well. Lot of different flavours especially on the platter.

Not Recommended for:

Nothing!! I haven't found anything so far.

So if you're a gastronomic adventure person this is a great place to go and highly recommended. You will keep coming back to nibble over the food and beer.

Author: Ravi Menon
Description:
The Good: Top floor is open on two sides and the tables by the side give you a nice view of the road where you can indulge in some people-watching. And yes - you can smoke! The music is soft (maybe a bit too soft as they don't turn up the volume when the place fills up). The beer is reasonably priced but isn't anything that I'd rave about. But it would at least satisfy my craving for microbrew ale in b'lore. All beers are far better than at Toit. But these guys didn't have their stout on the day I went.
I did a beer flight and then decided that none of the beers stood out particularly. I prefer hoppier ales. But these beers were all pretty all right. Nothing that anyone could take offense to (unlike at Toit - which however is a story that I'll reserve for Toit's review - let's just say that Toit's Chocolate stout tasted like watered down coffee). Btw, the wheat beer at Biereclub is better than the ale in terms of uniqueness - there is a nice lemony flavor to the aroma and the aftertaste.
The kebab platter with Moroccan chicken was excellent (3-4 pieces of each kebab and all of them juicy and succulent) and not bad value for money. It is probably a lot of food for two people unless you are really hungry. I just wish they'd given us a few more pita breads instead of just two. And some more salad greens would have been nice too.

The bad: Terrible to non-existent service worthy of the snootiest french restaurants I've been to. We sat down and the place was not even half-full. The waiters were getting menus off a ledge/ shelf next to us but didn't seem to acknowledge us. We kept giving them meaningful glances and even raised our hands to about shoulder level. Swiveled our chairs and stared at waiters moving across the room. They were sauntering around. I finally tapped a waiter on the shoulder as he grabbed a menu and asked if this section was "self-service". That got us the menus and an apology. But each time we wanted to place an order it was almost as bad.
And it wasn't just us who were being ignored. There was an European gent who had sat down at another table 5 mins after we got in. All of us were waiting for the waiters! The European gentleman actually got up and had to wave to a passing waiter to get the menu! I'd have been really annoyed if I hadn't been in a good Friday evening mood and was happy to sit, smoke and chat through the waiting.

I think that we just don't have people who understand the concept of unique brews is what makes microbrews interesting. Or perhaps we don't have good master brewers in India. I've had better amateur home-brew in the US than most beers at bangalore's microbrews. But if I had to choose between Toit and here, I'd head for the Biere Club in a heartbeat!

Author: Vivek
Description: Kool place to hangout with friends. Food, drink n crowd were awesome.

Author: Asia R.
Description: I love this place, I wish I lived closer so I could go there more often, decor is beautiful, cozy, the most friendly staff I ever met, strawberry margaritas are to die for, beer is also amazing, if you don't know what to choose you can always order beer samples, food is very tasty, maybe bit expensive but its worth it, great place to chill

Author: Abu Davis
Description:
I would like to share a funny thing that took place at the Biere Club. During my recent visit which was a saturday evening, there was a huge crowd and all the tables were reserved. I didn't care much about finding a seat, so stood there with my favorite - the STOUT. Then this tall guy (foreign national) walks in and asks for a table for 5 to a staff to which he politely replied "Sir, all tables are fully reserved, you may have to wait". Then this guy goes on to say " I will pay you a 500/- Rs. tip and ensure the final bill tonight wont be less than 20,000/-". And guess what, the staff didn't buy it, the guy had to wait some more time. I was awestruck! Good to know that the staff is unbiased & fair to all. Three cheers for the Biere Club :-)


----- 17 Aug, 2012 -----

I have tasted beers abroad, most of which are ALE's. And at the beire club, all of them taste somewhat close to the ones available in Germany and Belgium except for "Wheat" or "German hefeweizen". If the brew master is listening, let me tell you, you have no idea what it tastes like in Germany!!! Its totally different flavor altogether. Please do your homework on hefeweizen before your next brew :-)

P.S.: Please stop adding bananas to the hefeweizen, if you haven't already!! And please make the beers more affordable... I love your stout and Irish Red Ale (I tasted few months back, haven't seen it ever since, did you stop making them?)

Author: Badari
Description: Fantastic place, Awesome beer with some great food. I sure will keep coming back for more! Any plans to open up a place in south Bangalore? ;)

Author: Garima Singhal
Description: Went to Biere club with a huge group of friends. We had reserved the first floor seating as that provides a more restaurant like seating as opposed to the ground floor which is more like a bar (but I guess that's the point). None of us being beer drinkers and most of us vegetarians, we had an awesome time. The food was really good, and so were the cocktails, the Pom basil martini was absolutely delish. But I suppose for my group of friends who don't drink beer and aren't about to be converted, we kind of missed the point. But that's ok. It was AWESOME fun!! Thanks. The only comment was that even for a group of girls, the portions were really measly. The bread platter was dry, old bread, and one of the cocktails got mixed up.

Author: Ankit Chandel
Description: superbbbbbbbb bear.......... nice ambiance, lovely people, excellent service.....

Author: Vasavi Kandula
Description:
We went to the Biere Club on a sunday afternoon last month.. I love the location of Biere Club and absolutely loved the feel of the place.. (way better than TOIT, if you ask me!) The ground floor was almost full.. but we got a table immediately.. Had a Spanish Omlette and a sandwich..can't remember which.. both quite good..
Coming to the beer.. I found it just about ok.. nothing to rave about!

Author: Zubin
Description: THE BIERE CLUB!!, bangalore deserves this one!. This is just the place when you and your friends can sit-stand-lean drink, talk, laugh your lungs out, sing along and get drunk. We love this place.

Author: Manu
Description:
Having been thwarted once on a 'table for 2 after 7pm on Saturday' at TOIT, we decided to try another tack at The Biere Club and reserved a table via Poshvine for Sunday noon. We sat on the ground floor, with a view of Lavelle Road as well as the microbrewery. There are two more floors - the smoking sections, I think. Lots of graphics on the walls, reminded me a bit of Mario Miranda. Looks like a peaceful place, without the buzz that TOIT seems to have, but that was probably because of the time we were there at.

From the huge selection of beverages and meatables, (don't worry, there are veg options in plenty too) we decided to go for a wheat beer (you get a 500 ml for the price of 330 ml as part of a breakfast combo) and a glass of Biere Sangria. We also ordered a Moroccan Chicken to go with it. The wheat beer was fine, and perfect for the non-regular beeraholics, but on my taste buds scale I prefer the Basmati Blonde at TOIT and the one at Punjabi by Nature, in that order. The stout beer is pretty good, if we go by the 4sq tips. Maybe some other time, when I'm not riding back. The Sangria was much better, and chances are that you'll really like it - especially the little zing that the lager adds.

The Breakfastwich arrived first - a sub with ham, bacon, a couple of fried eggs and a token cheese presence. While the bacon and baked beans were just fine, the entire thing proved a bit unwieldy, and didn't really impress much. The Breakfast pizza arrived a bit later, and it was then that we realised that they had forgotten to tell the kitchen about the Moroccan chicken. But one look at the pizza was enough to convince us that it was a fortunate mistake. The thin crust pizza had bacon, sausages, mushrooms and a fried egg at the centre of it all. Sliced just right, it was quite awesome with a tomato tang, mild spice and a good load of cheese. Great combo with the beer. We really had no space for desserts.

The service was reasonably good, barring the Moroccan chicken incident that proved advantageous for us. All of the above cost us less than Rs.1200, thanks to the Poshvine deal. Definitely worth a visit, especially considering that it's Bangalore's first microbrewery.

Author: Alex Jacob
Description: The whole concept of the micro breweries are simply mind blowing. Biere Club stands up to the mark. The wheat beer here is a big favourite. The staff is very friendly and polite. Although, the food portions could be a little bigger for the price.

Author: Ikshita Mishra
Description: The city's first microbrewery, Biere Club is spread across three floors, serves six kinds of home brewed beer. Good Mediterranean food and great music add to the ambience.

Author: Vinay Shetty
Description:
You had better top up your pockets before you enter,cause the prices here are not on the lower side.

Having said that ,every penny spent will be worth it if you crave for something different than what the city has to offer.

I would insist you try all the samplers for the different varieties of biere on offer. Seasonal offer is a Mango Beer. Says it all really, can't think of another pub in bangalore serving up such concoctions other than Monkey Bar.

Soak in the ambiance for all it's worth and sit back and enjoy.

Food is very soulful, as in it's not dripping in spices, and does not negate the taste of your drink.

Location at the center of the City makes it all the more appealing.

Cons : More Waiters please! You do wanna make more money with more orders don't ya.

Author: Sromon Das
Description:
Decent microbrewery
Pretty decent place in B'lore. Very spacious, and nice ambience in general. Located bang opposite UB CIty. I had their seasonal beer (mango), which I found to be quite good. On the downside, the staff is not very prompt in their service.

Author: Himanshu
Description:
Good beer... Had the Wheat beer , tasted better than the wheat beer at TOIT, staff also better and more polite. Food was also good , good presentation. Good woodwork inside , interiors not as good as TOIT, but casual beer drinking environment.
Overall a good place to get beer drunk.

Author: Juhi
Description: Awesome and huge place with friendly staff, great drinks and a vast array of food items to choose from. Though I never tried the "bierre" as I am not a beer person but the martinis and cocktails were good. The music is decent as well. A must place to go for all the beer lovers!

Author: Amanda Fernandes
Description:
Amazing place to go to. The beer is just fabulous and you can really taste the difference between your normal kingfisher, bud etc. The food is scrumptious. We had ordered the crunchy chicken, sausages wrapped in bacon and prawns, each dish was cooked to perfection and all were equally mouth watering!!
And the Star of the day was the stand-up comedian Saurabh Pant who was completely hilarious. I hope they have more stand up comedians come there!!! I will definitely go.

Overall a fantastic experience! I highly recommend !!!

Author: Ashwin Ravichandran
Description:
I had been to the Biere Club recently with a friend and trust me, the experience was MIND-BLOWING!

Belgian Ale was on top of your minds with the highest alcohol content (9%). We had the Mixed Platter which was served with really tasty grub.
Hospitality was really nice. We were a bit late for our reservation and they called us up and confirmed. The waiters were courteous and kept coming back to ask whether we were enjoying the experience or not.

The only drawback to Biere Club are the prices. But, to maintain such a place, I bet it costs a pretty dime.

But, rest assured, this is the place to go if you want to have a killer time with your friends.

Biere Club :)

Author: Shruti Dwivedi
Description:
Beer lovers this is "THE" place to go.

Amazing place that serves a huge variety of beer. The music is great and so is the ambience. There are 3 floors in all, the top most being an open air,smoking zone.

The place is quite crowded during the weekends so be sure to get your reservations early. We did not explore the food there a lot but among starters the platters can be a good bet for both veg and non-veg.

The in-house DJ plays some great music and the crowd is one of the best in the city.Once you go there prepared to get hooked on.

Thumbs up!

Author: Vasu Bajaj
Description:
Superb place to unwind with top quality beers from around the world and home made beers. One of the best places in bangalore to unwind with BEER.
One word - superb.

Author: EKhan
Description:
Wanna drink great beer and chill out after a busy day. Then come to The Biere Club! There is nothing like beer brewed in the same building you are drinking in. The food menu is diverse and since I'm partial to Mediterranean food, I was in heaven.

There is a great crowd, warm ambiance, and affordable beer, so there is nothing more to ask for!

Cheers!

Author: Shounik.vig
Description: If u are beer crazy n wanna try new flavours of beer - Must go place!!

Author: Aperna Deb
Description:
Summary: A microbrewery which churns decent beer. Not recommended on a weekend night, unless prepared for a long wait. Maybe good for a larger party, and definitely not the place for a quiet drink.

The Biere club is the city's first (and only?) microbrewery, I am told. No wonder everyone wants to try it out. We landed there on a Saturday evening at around 9pm. The place was quite chaotic -- all the tables were occupied, and there was no one who could be contacted to know about the wait situation. We ended up standing most of the evening and finally got a table at around 10:15pm.

The place offers four kinds of beers - stout, lager, ale and wheat. They haven't given names to these, which I think is odd. I am a stout person, so much so that I didn't taste any of the others. (On a saner night I might've asked for a sampler). The drink was OK - it had a slight acidic tinge much like certain coffees. Indeed one of the members in party compared with the MTR filter coffee - a stretch, but not completely without reason. Overall, I would have it again at this place.

I can't comment on the food as we didn't have any, and I didn't look at the menu at all. The decor of the place is reminiscent of Cafe Mondegar in Bombay with one whole wall carrying a graffiti of people at the joint. At one point of time there was some fire juggling done by the bartenders, and at some other point a server dressed as a harlequin was walking around the tables. All in all, we had a fun night - but the fact that we were seven and two helped.

Author: Avinash
Description:
Micro-breweries are the essence of what brewing a beer stands for, innovation, robust flavor and a personal touch. Unfortunately, in India, we are rarely given the opportunity to experience the goodness the comes out of the experimentation that micro-breweries around the world are afforded to make. The Biere Club, by advertising itself as Bangalore's first micro-brewery, has tried to put an end to that and the lack of originality that is seeping through Â the "pub capital of India" when it comes to beer, and, boy, did they knock it out off the park.Â 

The Biere Club, located at the intersection of Lavelle Road and Vittal Mallya Road, is three floors of beery goodness.Â The ambience of TBC can be described with one simple word, fun. That's the vibe you get when you first open it's doors. With it's brightly lit lounging area decorated by cartoon murals of beer chuggers, TBC provides you with an inviting atmosphere to enjoy your freshly brewed beer. Privacy is thrown out the window here and who really needs it anyway when you are there with your fellow beer inhalers.Â 

We made the mistake of not reserving a table beforehand thinking who would be at a place like this at 7 on a Sunday evening. We were sorely mistaken. We had to make do in the non smoking section in the first floor(smoking section is on the third floor) and had to scrounge up a couple of seats near the entrance. It really didn't make much of a difference to me because I can drink beer on the street, but for others it might pose a problem. So, if you do decide to check out this place, RESERVE your table.Â 

Moving on to the beer, TBC provides you with 4 main options of beer; lager, ale, stout and wheat along with couple of other seasonal brews. Since I've been on a quest to find stout beer in Bangalore, I happily ordered the stout without even looking at the menu. Unfortunately for me, they were out of stout, which almost made me yell out in anger. It is understandable since they brew their own beer in limited quantities and stout apparently is their most requested. My next immediate choice was wheat since we do live in the lager capital of the world. When we first received our big mug of beer, we could point out the difference between this one and bottled beer without even tasting it, the freshness and the aroma was almost overwhelming and my partner immediately declared it as the best beer she has ever tasted. Pulling off a good pint of wheat beer is difficult in that some taste too chalky but this one was just perfect; creamy, frothy and rich. I would put it in my personal top 5 of wheat beers that I've tasted. The cost of the beer made it even more enticing for us (4 mugs for a total of 800).Â 

The priority here is on the beer and not the food. The food had a distinct middle eastern touch to it but was definitely Â hit or miss, at least for me personally. We ordered the vegetarian kebab platter, which came with roasted eggplant and bell peppers, paneer tikka, charred mushrooms, and potato rolls of some kind along with two variety of sauces. The sauces were tasteless and other items on the well decorated wooden platter was Â average. For 520 bucks, we expected a bit more. I had to order some meat to accompany my beer which nudged me towards the sliced beef panini served with French fries and salad. The panini was prepared just the way I expect, toasted on the outside with the soft beef filling just exploding in your mouth. Highly recommended. We finished it off with baklava served with vanilla ice cream and it was a big hit as well.Â 

The service was excellent. The person assisting us with our order was extremely patient while we figured out what to order and was courteous enough to bring us our cell phones, which we accidentally left on our table, when we were standing outside for a smoke. The service had a very personal touch to it, just like their beers. The food was interestingly presented as well and as they say we eat with our eyes first.Â 

Whether you are looking for the clean and crisp taste of the Â lager or the dark and roasted taste of the stout (if they have it), it is all provided to you in a neat little package at TBC. Beer is the starter, main course and dessert here and I wouldn't want it any other way. So, chuck those tasteless kingfisher bottles and check out what a freshly brewed beer does to your taste buds.

Items ordered -

Drinks - Wheat Beer (4 mugs - 330ml)
Food - Vegetarian Kebab Platter & Sliced Beef Panini
Dessert - Baklava with Vanilla Ice-cream

Total Cost - 2150

Author: Jainiti Singh
Description: Lovely Beer.  Good selection.  Nice staff. Very nice casual ambience.  Wish their chairs were a little more comfortable!

Author: Foodie First
Description:
Call it the 'Leopold Cafe' of Bengaluru or a 'I can do better than you Koshys!' kind of place, The Biere Club deserves a thumbs up - for their delicious beers, the ambience and the guts to bring up a micro-brewery right smack in Mallaya territory.
Be that as it may, my friend (a reluctant Banglorian, who is non-trusting of anything 'new' in Bengaluru) and I enjoyed a lovely afternoon sipping our Golden Ale with the light drizzle in the backdrop offering a great ambience.
Sometimes you want to go to a place, catch up over a drink and have a conversation. A kind where you can hear each other without screaming yourself hoarse and silly. The Biere Club allowed us this and more, with the surprisingly refreshing playlist of oldies and the unobtrusive service.
What was meant to be a quick beer and out turned out to be a 4-hour-long pit stop, one that'll be a regular fare as far as we're concerned.

Foodies First

Author: Daniel Thomas
Description: Beers are mediocre at best, food is no great shakes, and we left it at that, signed our debit card slip and called it a night. Next day we received a message, for duplicate charges from our bank. Got to the bank and got the passbook updated with the duplicate charges clearly detailed. Alarmed with this, we reached Biere Club at 11am, spoke to staff on duty, then a couple of managers, and after a frustrating half hour finally a Mr Vijay Kumar, who announced he was the owner and we will have to follow their procedure for the refund, which meant accompanying 2 clueless staff members to the HDFC branch on MG road, 12.40pm at the bank the manager could not find duplicate credits on their account, we were advised to contact our bank and dispute the charges. 2 hours of aggravation and frustration, if the bill amount had been any smaller I would have just walked away, Simply put, the Biere Club was a just a disappointment, which the management and block headed cashiering staff have successfully turned into bitterness. If you do visit, do not use your credit cards or debit cards.

Author: Akanksha Sehgal
Description:
Me and my friends had heard alot about this place. So we thought of going to this new place. Its right at the entrance of Lavelle road opposite to Sunny's or the Mocha road. If its a weekend (or when I went, even a weekday), make sure you reserve your table prior, if you don't want to end up spending your time in front of the bartender. There's a floor where smoking is allowed as well. The place is very lively and has an Irish pub theme. The menu when I visited was limited. They had Lager, Stout, Ale and Wheat. Stout was the special thing for everyone here. These people have their very own micro brewery, which is visible to all from inside. Their beer tastes unique and good, the Stout though is a bit stronger than the rest and has a bitter-ish unique taste. They have flavored beers too, from which only Wheat was available, which tasted really awesome. In short , their beers are really great and I couldn't point anything weird about them. For food , we ordered Chicken Wings , which sucked to the core. So that is a thing to avoid. They have nightcaps and cocktails as well , I heard from my women friends that they were good.
So if you feel like grabbing a quick fresh brewed beer , this is your choice , though you are unlikely to get a seat anywhere else other than that in front of the bartender (which still is fun).

Great place , thumbs up , a must to go if you're a beer freak and are in Bangalore.

Author: Vikas Alva
Description: Last week a bunch of friends and I figured we'd hit Hard Rock Cafe since it'd been a while we'd grabbed a beer. Turned out we couldn't get ourselves in. Patience is not something we guys take pride in. So we leave and just as we get closer to UB City, we see a new place right opposite the restaurant Sunny's on Lavelle road. It looked pretty cool from outside so we decide to give it a try. It was called The Biere Club. Three floors of awesomeness. We picked the third, smoking area. Done up pretty well, this pub. Very Irish. To our surprise they apparently had their own micro-brewery. I made up my mind to try out every type they had but unfortunately, they had only Lager, Ale and Stout. But after a while they ran after Stout, which was the most popular brew that night I suppose. The beer was delicious and crisp. The Stout was much milder than what I had heard of but the Lager and Ale were stronger and kept flowing. The crowd was very lively and young. This is the kind of place where even girls would be most comfortable coming and enjoying a nice mug of beer without hassle, that's what my women friends said so I'll just take their word. We didn't actually order much food besides the French Fries and Chicken wings. Fries are fries, how wrong could they go there. But the wings were a big big mistake. Downright bad. All in all, a great place to hang out with friends and enjoy some freshly brewed beer.
